Table of contents

  • 1 Packages in a reproducible environment
  • 2 Set working directory
  • 3 Biofilm endpoints
    • Chlorophyll and ash free dry weight of biofilm after colonisation + chronic contamination of 14 days
    • 3.1 Chlorophyll
    • 3.2 AFDW after colonisation
  • 4 Feeding rate snails and ash free dry weight after feeding
    • 4.1 Feeding rate snails
    • 4.2 AFDW after feeding
  • 5 Dryweight and growth snails
  • 6 Fatty acid composition snails
  • 7 Fatty acid composition biofilm
  • 8 Temperature and light conditions
    • 8.1 Biofilm colonisation and contamination
    • 8.2 Feeding assay

Bottom-up effects of an herbicide and an antibiotic on Potamopyrgus antipodarum

Sophie Oster, Verena C. Schreiner, Tobias Schmitt, Patrick Fink, Markus Weitere, Ibrahim Araoye, Ralf Schulz, Mirco Bundschuh

R script written by Sophie Oster
checked by Verena C. Schreiner


RPTU Kaiserslautern-Landau
Fortstrasse 7
76829 Landau in der Pfalz
GERMANY

This study was conducted with four treatments and three time points explanatory variables depending on the endpoint:

  • treatment (categorical)
    • uncontaminated control (negative control, NC)
    • herbicide propyzamide (Pro)
    • antibiotic ciprofloxacin (Cip)
    • mixture of propyzamide and ciprofloxacin (Mix)
  • time.point (categorical)
    • week 1
    • week 2
    • week 3

1 Packages in a reproducible environment

# renv::init() 
# renv::snapshot()
# renv::status()

library(stats)
library(rstatix)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyr)
library(broom)
library(dunn.test)
library(patchwork)
library(vegan)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggforce)
library(concaveman)
library(gridExtra)
library(stringr)
library(shades)
library(ggpubr)
library(base)
library(ggokabeito)
library(multcomp)
library(car)
library(asbio)
library(plotrix)
library(lmtest)
library(boot)
library(moments)
library(lme4)
library(DAAG)
library(performance)
library(emmeans)
library(purrr)
library(DT)
library(pairwiseAdonis)

2 Set working directory

prj <- getwd()
setwd(prj)

3 Biofilm endpoints

Chlorophyll and ash free dry weight of biofilm after colonisation + chronic contamination of 14 days

  • Unit chlorophyll a, b & c: µg chlorophyll / cm²

  • Unit afdw after colonisation: mg / cm²

# insert data
biofilm_endpoints <-
  read.table(file.path(prj, "data\\biofilm_endpoints.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(biofilm_endpoints)

3.1 Chlorophyll

Caclulate mean chla, chlb + chlc of each treatment with distinction of the weeks:

#chl a
mean_chlorophyll_a <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(week, treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_chlorophyll_a = mean(chla))

print(mean_chlorophyll_a)
# A tibble: 12 × 3
# Groups:   week [3]
    week treatment mean_chlorophyll_a
   <int> <chr>                  <dbl>
 1     1 Cip                   0.157 
 2     1 Mix                   0.189 
 3     1 NC                    0.154 
 4     1 Pro                   0.191 
 5     2 Cip                   0.0954
 6     2 Mix                   0.0317
 7     2 NC                    0.181 
 8     2 Pro                   0.0920
 9     3 Cip                   0.250 
10     3 Mix                   0.680 
11     3 NC                    0.586 
12     3 Pro                   0.629 
# chl b
mean_chlorophyll_b <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(week, treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_chlorophyll_b = mean(chlb))

print(mean_chlorophyll_b)
# A tibble: 12 × 3
# Groups:   week [3]
    week treatment mean_chlorophyll_b
   <int> <chr>                  <dbl>
 1     1 Cip                   0.0434
 2     1 Mix                   0.0773
 3     1 NC                    0.0443
 4     1 Pro                   0.0487
 5     2 Cip                   0.0442
 6     2 Mix                   0.0242
 7     2 NC                    0.0370
 8     2 Pro                   0.0353
 9     3 Cip                   0.0661
10     3 Mix                   0.127 
11     3 NC                    0.105 
12     3 Pro                   0.0964
# chl c
mean_chlorophyll_c <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(week, treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_chlorophyll_c = mean(chlc))

print(mean_chlorophyll_c)
# A tibble: 12 × 3
# Groups:   week [3]
    week treatment mean_chlorophyll_c
   <int> <chr>                  <dbl>
 1     1 Cip                   0.0481
 2     1 Mix                   0.0958
 3     1 NC                    0.0468
 4     1 Pro                   0.0522
 5     2 Cip                   0.0535
 6     2 Mix                   0.0319
 7     2 NC                    0.0341
 8     2 Pro                   0.0440
 9     3 Cip                   0.0479
10     3 Mix                   0.0972
11     3 NC                    0.0880
12     3 Pro                   0.0858

Now calculate mean chla, chlb + chlc of each treatment without taking into account the different weekly colonizations:

#chl a
mean_chlorophyll_a_treatment <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_chlorophyll_a_treatment = mean(chla))

print(mean_chlorophyll_a_treatment)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_chlorophyll_a_treatment
  <chr>                            <dbl>
1 Cip                              0.167
2 Mix                              0.300
3 NC                               0.307
4 Pro                              0.304
# chl b
mean_chlorophyll_b_treatment <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_chlorophyll_b_treatment = mean(chlb))

print(mean_chlorophyll_b_treatment)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_chlorophyll_b_treatment
  <chr>                            <dbl>
1 Cip                             0.0513
2 Mix                             0.0761
3 NC                              0.0621
4 Pro                             0.0601
# chl c
mean_chlorophyll_c_treatment <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_chlorophyll_c_treatment = mean(chlc))

print(mean_chlorophyll_c_treatment)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_chlorophyll_c_treatment
  <chr>                            <dbl>
1 Cip                             0.0498
2 Mix                             0.0750
3 NC                              0.0563
4 Pro                             0.0607

Histogram chlorophyll a, b + c

# chl a
hist(biofilm_endpoints $ chla) 

skewness(biofilm_endpoints $ chla, na.rm = TRUE)
[1] 1.601648
shapiro.test(biofilm_endpoints $ chla)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  biofilm_endpoints$chla
W = 0.78095, p-value = 6.966e-06
# chl b
hist(biofilm_endpoints $ chlb) 

skewness(biofilm_endpoints $ chlb, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 1.452618
shapiro.test(biofilm_endpoints $ chlb)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  biofilm_endpoints$chlb
W = 0.87043, p-value = 0.0005833
# chl c
hist(biofilm_endpoints $ chlc) 

skewness(biofilm_endpoints $ chlc, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 1.253578
shapiro.test(biofilm_endpoints $ chlc)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  biofilm_endpoints$chlc
W = 0.89434, p-value = 0.002398

All not normal distributed, right skewed but skewness < 1.5 therefore no data transformation for model

Model chla

Linear model with treatment and week and their interaction (week:treatment) as explanatory variables.

# week and treatment as factor
biofilm_endpoints $ week <- factor(biofilm_endpoints $ week)
biofilm_endpoints $ treatment <- factor(biofilm_endpoints $ treatment)

model_chla <- lm(chla ~ week + treatment + week:treatment, data = biofilm_endpoints)

par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(model_chla) # point cloud looks evenly distributed --> normal distribution

qreference(residuals(model_chla)) 

drop1(model_chla, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
chla ~ week + treatment + week:treatment
               Df Sum of Sq     RSS     AIC F value Pr(>F)
<none>                      0.67296 -119.27               
week:treatment  6   0.25244 0.92540 -119.80  1.5005 0.2204

Now check differences between treatments within weeks.

chla_inter <- lsmeans(model_chla, ~ treatment | week )
chla_inter_dunn1 <- contrast(chla_inter, 'pairwise')
(chla_inter_dunn_sum1 <- summary(chla_inter_dunn1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt'))
week = 1:
 contrast  estimate    SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix -0.03246 0.137 24   -0.410  0.34477  -0.237  0.9952
 Cip - NC   0.00243 0.137 24   -0.375  0.37965   0.018  1.0000
 Cip - Pro -0.03374 0.137 24   -0.411  0.34349  -0.247  0.9946
 Mix - NC   0.03488 0.137 24   -0.342  0.41211   0.255  0.9940
 Mix - Pro -0.00128 0.137 24   -0.379  0.37594  -0.009  1.0000
 NC - Pro  -0.03617 0.137 24   -0.413  0.34106  -0.265  0.9933

week = 2:
 contrast  estimate    SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix  0.06371 0.137 24   -0.314  0.44101   0.466  0.9658
 Cip - NC  -0.08519 0.137 24   -0.462  0.29210  -0.623  0.9237
 Cip - Pro  0.00341 0.137 24   -0.374  0.38070   0.025  1.0000
 Mix - NC  -0.14890 0.137 24   -0.526  0.22839  -1.089  0.6994
 Mix - Pro -0.06031 0.137 24   -0.438  0.31699  -0.441  0.9707
 NC - Pro   0.08860 0.137 24   -0.289  0.46589   0.648  0.9152

week = 3:
 contrast  estimate    SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix -0.42970 0.137 24   -0.807 -0.05238  -3.143  0.0211
 Cip - NC  -0.33589 0.137 24   -0.713  0.04143  -2.457  0.0929
 Cip - Pro -0.37888 0.137 24   -0.756 -0.00155  -2.771  0.0487
 Mix - NC   0.09381 0.137 24   -0.284  0.47113   0.686  0.9014
 Mix - Pro  0.05083 0.137 24   -0.326  0.42815   0.372  0.9820
 NC - Pro  -0.04298 0.137 24   -0.420  0.33434  -0.314  0.9890

Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 6 estimates 
P value adjustment: mvt method for 6 tests

No significant differences of the treatments within week 1 and 2. Only Cip-Mix and Cip-Pro significant in week 2.

Now check for differences between weeks.

anova(model_chla)
Analysis of Variance Table

Response: chla
               Df  Sum Sq Mean Sq F value    Pr(>F)    
week            2 1.31154 0.65577 23.3871 2.312e-06 ***
treatment       3 0.12552 0.04184  1.4922    0.2419    
week:treatment  6 0.25244 0.04207  1.5005    0.2204    
Residuals      24 0.67296 0.02804                      
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# week variable highly significant (p=7.689e-08)

chla_inter2 <- lsmeans(model_chla, ~ week |  treatment)
chla_inter_dunn2 <- contrast(chla_inter2, 'pairwise')
(chla_inter_dunn_sum2 <- summary(chla_inter_dunn2, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt'))
treatment = Cip:
 contrast      estimate    SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2   0.0614 0.137 24   -0.280   0.4028   0.449  0.8952
 week1 - week3  -0.0933 0.137 24   -0.435   0.2480  -0.683  0.7757
 week2 - week3  -0.1548 0.137 24   -0.496   0.1866  -1.132  0.5043

treatment = Mix:
 contrast      estimate    SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2   0.1576 0.137 24   -0.184   0.4991   1.153  0.4920
 week1 - week3  -0.4906 0.137 24   -0.832  -0.1491  -3.588  0.0042
 week2 - week3  -0.6482 0.137 24   -0.990  -0.3067  -4.741  0.0002

treatment = NC:
 contrast      estimate    SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2  -0.0262 0.137 24   -0.368   0.3152  -0.192  0.9800
 week1 - week3  -0.4317 0.137 24   -0.773  -0.0903  -3.157  0.0113
 week2 - week3  -0.4055 0.137 24   -0.747  -0.0641  -2.966  0.0179

treatment = Pro:
 contrast      estimate    SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2   0.0986 0.137 24   -0.243   0.4399   0.721  0.7535
 week1 - week3  -0.4385 0.137 24   -0.780  -0.0971  -3.207  0.0101
 week2 - week3  -0.5371 0.137 24   -0.878  -0.1957  -3.928  0.0017

Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 3 estimates 
P value adjustment: mvt method for 3 tests
  • NC
    • Week 1 & 2 similar
    • Week 2 & 3 similar
    • difference between week 1 & 3
  • Cip
    • Week 1 & 2 similar
    • Week 1 & 3 similar
    • difference between week 2 & 3
  • Pro
    • Week 1 & 2 similar
    • differences between week 1 & 3 and 2 & 3
  • Mix
    • all weeks different

Week 1 & 2 quite similar, expect of Mix treatment. Week 3 quite different to week 1 & 2 despite of Cip treatment.

Model chlb

model_chlb <- lm(chlb ~ week + treatment + week:treatment, data = biofilm_endpoints)

par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(model_chlb) # point cloud looks evenly distributed --> normal distribution

qreference(residuals(model_chlb)) # normal distribution assumed

drop1(model_chlb, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
chlb ~ week + treatment + week:treatment
               Df Sum of Sq      RSS     AIC F value Pr(>F)
<none>                      0.022334 -241.87               
week:treatment  6 0.0057745 0.028108 -245.59  1.0342 0.4279

No significant differences. Still check for differences between treatments.

chlb_inter <- lsmeans(model_chlb, ~ treatment | week )
chlb_inter_dunn1 <- contrast(chlb_inter, 'pairwise')
(chlb_inter_dunn_sum1 <- summary(chlb_inter_dunn1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt'))
week = 1:
 contrast   estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix -0.033921 0.0249 24  -0.1027   0.0348  -1.362  0.5343
 Cip - NC  -0.000874 0.0249 24  -0.0696   0.0679  -0.035  1.0000
 Cip - Pro -0.005255 0.0249 24  -0.0740   0.0635  -0.211  0.9966
 Mix - NC   0.033048 0.0249 24  -0.0357   0.1018   1.327  0.5554
 Mix - Pro  0.028666 0.0249 24  -0.0401   0.0974   1.151  0.6625
 NC - Pro  -0.004382 0.0249 24  -0.0731   0.0644  -0.176  0.9980

week = 2:
 contrast   estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix  0.020001 0.0249 24  -0.0488   0.0888   0.803  0.8523
 Cip - NC   0.007282 0.0249 24  -0.0615   0.0761   0.292  0.9911
 Cip - Pro  0.008957 0.0249 24  -0.0598   0.0778   0.360  0.9837
 Mix - NC  -0.012719 0.0249 24  -0.0815   0.0561  -0.511  0.9557
 Mix - Pro -0.011044 0.0249 24  -0.0798   0.0578  -0.443  0.9703
 NC - Pro   0.001675 0.0249 24  -0.0671   0.0705   0.067  0.9999

week = 3:
 contrast   estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix -0.060688 0.0249 24  -0.1294   0.0080  -2.437  0.0967
 Cip - NC  -0.038809 0.0249 24  -0.1075   0.0299  -1.558  0.4205
 Cip - Pro -0.030338 0.0249 24  -0.0990   0.0383  -1.218  0.6218
 Mix - NC   0.021879 0.0249 24  -0.0468   0.0906   0.878  0.8159
 Mix - Pro  0.030350 0.0249 24  -0.0383   0.0990   1.219  0.6215
 NC - Pro   0.008471 0.0249 24  -0.0602   0.0772   0.340  0.9861

Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 6 estimates 
P value adjustment: mvt method for 6 tests

No differences between treatments for chlb.

Now check for differences between weeks.

anova(model_chlb)
Analysis of Variance Table

Response: chlb
               Df    Sum Sq   Mean Sq F value    Pr(>F)    
week            2 0.0255307 0.0127654 13.7176 0.0001065 ***
treatment       3 0.0028604 0.0009535  1.0246 0.3992539    
week:treatment  6 0.0057745 0.0009624  1.0342 0.4278661    
Residuals      24 0.0223339 0.0009306                      
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# week variable highly significant (p=5.259e-05)

chlb_inter2 <- lsmeans(model_chlb, ~ week |  treatment)
chlb_inter_dunn3 <- contrast(chlb_inter2, 'pairwise')
(chlb_inter_dunn_sum3 <- summary(chlb_inter_dunn3, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt'))
treatment = Cip:
 contrast       estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2 -0.000818 0.0249 24 -0.06300  0.06136  -0.033  0.9994
 week1 - week3 -0.022661 0.0249 24 -0.08484  0.03952  -0.910  0.6395
 week2 - week3 -0.021843 0.0249 24 -0.08402  0.04034  -0.877  0.6597

treatment = Mix:
 contrast       estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2  0.053105 0.0249 24 -0.00906  0.11527   2.132  0.1046
 week1 - week3 -0.049428 0.0249 24 -0.11159  0.01274  -1.984  0.1379
 week2 - week3 -0.102533 0.0249 24 -0.16470 -0.04037  -4.117  0.0012

treatment = NC:
 contrast       estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2  0.007338 0.0249 24 -0.05483  0.06951   0.295  0.9534
 week1 - week3 -0.060597 0.0249 24 -0.12277  0.00157  -2.433  0.0573
 week2 - week3 -0.067935 0.0249 24 -0.13010 -0.00577  -2.727  0.0305

treatment = Pro:
 contrast       estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2  0.013395 0.0249 24 -0.04878  0.07557   0.538  0.8536
 week1 - week3 -0.047744 0.0249 24 -0.10992  0.01443  -1.917  0.1556
 week2 - week3 -0.061138 0.0249 24 -0.12331  0.00103  -2.455  0.0546

Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 3 estimates 
P value adjustment: mvt method for 3 tests

Similar results/trends as for chla. Week 1 & 2 quite similar; differences in:

  • Pro treatment: week 2 & 3

  • Mix treatment: week 1 & 2; 2 & 3

Model chlc

model_chlc <- lm(chlc ~ week + treatment + week:treatment, data = biofilm_endpoints)

par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(model_chlc) # point cloud looks evenly distributed --> normal distribution

qreference(residuals(model_chlc)) # normal distribution assumed

drop1(model_chlc, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
chlc ~ week + treatment + week:treatment
               Df Sum of Sq      RSS     AIC F value Pr(>F)
<none>                      0.023816 -239.55               
week:treatment  6 0.0070417 0.030857 -242.23  1.1827 0.3485

No significant differences. Still check for differences between treatments.

chlc_inter <- lsmeans(model_chlc, ~ treatment | week )
chlc_inter_dunn1 <- contrast(chlc_inter, 'pairwise')
(chlc_inter_dunn_sum1 <- summary(chlc_inter_dunn1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt'))
week = 1:
 contrast  estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix -0.04766 0.0257 24  -0.1187   0.0233  -1.853  0.2743
 Cip - NC   0.00128 0.0257 24  -0.0697   0.0723   0.050  1.0000
 Cip - Pro -0.00412 0.0257 24  -0.0751   0.0669  -0.160  0.9985
 Mix - NC   0.04894 0.0257 24  -0.0221   0.1199   1.903  0.2535
 Mix - Pro  0.04354 0.0257 24  -0.0275   0.1145   1.693  0.3492
 NC - Pro  -0.00540 0.0257 24  -0.0764   0.0656  -0.210  0.9966

week = 2:
 contrast  estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix  0.02154 0.0257 24  -0.0495   0.0926   0.837  0.8362
 Cip - NC   0.01936 0.0257 24  -0.0517   0.0904   0.753  0.8747
 Cip - Pro  0.00943 0.0257 24  -0.0616   0.0805   0.367  0.9827
 Mix - NC  -0.00218 0.0257 24  -0.0732   0.0689  -0.085  0.9998
 Mix - Pro -0.01210 0.0257 24  -0.0831   0.0589  -0.471  0.9648
 NC - Pro  -0.00992 0.0257 24  -0.0810   0.0611  -0.386  0.9800

week = 3:
 contrast  estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix -0.04929 0.0257 24  -0.1202   0.0217  -1.916  0.2480
 Cip - NC  -0.04010 0.0257 24  -0.1110   0.0308  -1.559  0.4199
 Cip - Pro -0.03785 0.0257 24  -0.1088   0.0331  -1.471  0.4694
 Mix - NC   0.00919 0.0257 24  -0.0617   0.0801   0.357  0.9840
 Mix - Pro  0.01144 0.0257 24  -0.0595   0.0824   0.445  0.9700
 NC - Pro   0.00225 0.0257 24  -0.0687   0.0732   0.088  0.9998

Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 6 estimates 
P value adjustment: mvt method for 6 tests

No differences between treatments for chlorophyll c. Now check for differences between weeks:

anova(model_chlc)
Analysis of Variance Table

Response: chlc
               Df    Sum Sq   Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)  
week            2 0.0090577 0.0045289  4.5639 0.0209 *
treatment       3 0.0030658 0.0010219  1.0299 0.3970  
week:treatment  6 0.0070417 0.0011736  1.1827 0.3485  
Residuals      24 0.0238157 0.0009923                 
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# week variable significant (p=0.01332)

chlc_inter2 <- lsmeans(model_chlc, ~ week |  treatment)
chlc_inter_dunn3 <- contrast(chlc_inter2, 'pairwise')
(chlc_inter_dunn_sum3 <- summary(chlc_inter_dunn3, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt'))
treatment = Cip:
 contrast       estimate     SE df  lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2 -0.005356 0.0257 24 -0.069605  0.05889  -0.208  0.9764
 week1 - week3  0.000182 0.0257 24 -0.064067  0.06443   0.007  1.0000
 week2 - week3  0.005538 0.0257 24 -0.058711  0.06979   0.215  0.9748

treatment = Mix:
 contrast       estimate     SE df  lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2  0.063842 0.0257 24 -0.000423  0.12811   2.482  0.0517
 week1 - week3 -0.001444 0.0257 24 -0.065708  0.06282  -0.056  0.9983
 week2 - week3 -0.065285 0.0257 24 -0.129550 -0.00102  -2.538  0.0458

treatment = NC:
 contrast       estimate     SE df  lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2  0.012724 0.0257 24 -0.051523  0.07697   0.495  0.8745
 week1 - week3 -0.041191 0.0257 24 -0.105438  0.02306  -1.602  0.2643
 week2 - week3 -0.053915 0.0257 24 -0.118162  0.01033  -2.096  0.1119

treatment = Pro:
 contrast       estimate     SE df  lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2  0.008198 0.0257 24 -0.056053  0.07245   0.319  0.9457
 week1 - week3 -0.033542 0.0257 24 -0.097792  0.03071  -1.304  0.4066
 week2 - week3 -0.041740 0.0257 24 -0.105990  0.02251  -1.623  0.2556

Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 3 estimates 
P value adjustment: mvt method for 3 tests
  • NC
    • all weeks similar
  • Cip
    • all weeks similar
  • Pro
    • all weeks similar
  • Mix
    • Week 1 & 2, and 2 & 3 different

Boxplots chlorophyll a, b + c

# chlorophyll a
chla <- ggplot(data = biofilm_endpoints, 
               mapping = aes(x = factor(week), 
                             y = chla, 
                             fill = factor(treatment, levels = c("NC", "Cip", "Pro", "Mix")))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) + 
  scale_color_okabe_ito() + 
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = NULL , y = expression("Chlorophyll a (µg/cm"^"2"*")"), fill = "Treatment") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Week 1", "Week 2", "Week 3"))

chla 

# save the plot
ggsave(chla, 
       filename = "chla.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image

3.2 AFDW after colonisation

Calculate mean AFDW of each treatment with distinction of the 3 weeks.

mean_afdw_after_colonisation <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(week, treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_afdw_after_colonisation = mean(afdw_after_colonisation))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'week'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.

Now calculate mean AFDW of each treatment without taking into account the different weekly colonizations:

mean_afdw_after_colonisation_treatments <- biofilm_endpoints %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_afdw_after_colonisation_treatments = mean(afdw_after_colonisation))

print(mean_afdw_after_colonisation_treatments)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_afdw_after_colonisation_treatments
  <fct>                                       <dbl>
1 Cip                                         0.125
2 Mix                                         0.142
3 NC                                          0.273
4 Pro                                         0.226

Histogram AFDW after colonisation

hist(biofilm_endpoints $ afdw_after_colonisation) 

skewness(biofilm_endpoints $ afdw_after_colonisation, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 1.433642
shapiro.test(biofilm_endpoints $ afdw_after_colonisation)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  biofilm_endpoints$afdw_after_colonisation
W = 0.759, p-value = 2.751e-06

Not normal distributed, right skewed but skewness <1.5 therefore no data transformation for model.

Model AFDW after colonisation

Linear model with treatment and week and their interaction (week:treatment) as explanatory variables.

model_afdw_after_colonisation <- lm(afdw_after_colonisation ~ week + treatment + week:treatment, data = biofilm_endpoints)

par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(model_afdw_after_colonisation) # not perfectly even distributed, but ok

qreference(residuals(model_afdw_after_colonisation)) # normal distribution assumed

drop1(model_afdw_after_colonisation, test = 'F')
Single term deletions

Model:
afdw_after_colonisation ~ week + treatment + week:treatment
               Df Sum of Sq     RSS     AIC F value  Pr(>F)  
<none>                      0.16110 -170.73                  
week:treatment  6   0.13914 0.30024 -160.32  3.4546 0.01328 *
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Significant difference for week:treatment. Check differences between treatments within weeks.

afdw_after_colonisation_inter <- lsmeans(model_afdw_after_colonisation, ~ treatment | week )

afdw_after_colonisation_inter_dunn1 <- contrast(afdw_after_colonisation_inter, 'pairwise')

(afdw_after_colonisation_inter_dunn_sum1 <- summary(afdw_after_colonisation_inter_dunn1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt'))

 contrast  estimate     SE df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value

 Cip - Pro -0.05659 0.0669 24  -0.2409  0.12777  -0.846  0.8321
 Mix - NC  -0.01705 0.0669 24  -0.2014  0.16731  -0.255  0.9940

week = 3:

Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 6 estimates

Significant differences in week 3 Cip-NC, Cip-Pro, Mix-NC and Mix-Pro.

Now check for differences within weeks.

anova(model_afdw_after_colonisation)

# week variable highly significant

Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 3 estimates 
P value adjustment: mvt method for 3 tests

  • Cip

    • Difference between week 1 and 3

  • Mix

    • Week 1 & 2 similar; week 1 & 3 and 2 & 3 different.

  • NC

    • all weeks different from each other

  • Pro

    • Week 1 & 2 similar; week 1 & 3 and 2 & 3 different.

Boxplot AFDW after colonisation

afdw_after_colonisation_boxplot <- ggplot(data = biofilm_endpoints, 
               mapping = aes(x = factor(week), 
                             y = afdw_after_colonisation, 
                             fill = factor(treatment, levels = c("NC", "Cip", "Pro", "Mix")))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) + 
  scale_color_okabe_ito() + 
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = NULL , y = expression("Ash free dry weight (mg/cm"^"2"*")"), fill = "Treatment") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Week 1", "Week 2", "Week 3"))

afdw_after_colonisation_boxplot 

# save the plot
ggsave(afdw_after_colonisation_boxplot, 
       filename = "afdw_after_colonisation.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')

4 Feeding rate snails and ash free dry weight after feeding

  • NA’s due to missing snail and zero values of feeding rate due to no measurable feeding in respective week

  • Unit feeding rate: µg / day and unit ash free dry weight (afdw): mg / cm²

  • Column “used_for_FA_analysis” highlights the ten snails used for FA analyses for each treatment

# insert data
afdw_feeding_rate <-
  read.table(file.path(prj, "data\\feeding_assay_afdw_feeding_rate.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(afdw_feeding_rate)

4.1 Feeding rate snails

Histogram feeding rate

hist(afdw_feeding_rate $ feeding_rate) 

skewness(afdw_feeding_rate $ feeding_rate, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 3.865973
shapiro.test(afdw_feeding_rate $ feeding_rate)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  afdw_feeding_rate$feeding_rate
W = 0.54728, p-value < 2.2e-16

Feeding rate variable not normal distributed, right skewed and skewness > 2 -> log10 transformation for model

Check for outliers:

#Run function one time, after that you can use the shortcut outlierKD (your subset, variable)
outlierKD_feeding_rate <- function(dt, id_col = "treatment", start_col = 6, end_col = 6) {
  # Initialize an empty list to store results for each column
  outlier_results <- list()
  
  
  for (i in start_col:end_col) {
    col_name <- colnames(dt)[i]  # Get the column name
    
    # Extract the column values
    var_name <- dt[[col_name]]
    
    # Check if the column is numeric
    if (is.numeric(var_name)) {
      na1 <- sum(is.na(var_name))
      m1 <- mean(var_name, na.rm = TRUE)
      
      # Identify outliers using boxplot stats
      outlier <- boxplot.stats(var_name)$out  
      
      # Find rows with outliers
      outlier_rows <- which(var_name %in% outlier)
      
      # If no outliers, skip the rest of the loop
      if (length(outlier_rows) == 0) next
      
      # Get replicate values for the outliers
      id_values <- dt[[id_col]][outlier_rows]
      
      # Mean of outliers
      mo <- mean(outlier)
      
      # Collect the outlier information for this column
      outlier_results[[col_name]] <- data.frame(
        Column = col_name,
        Row = outlier_rows,
        Replicate = id_values,
        Outlier = outlier
      )
      
      cat("\nOutliers for column:", col_name, "\n")
      cat("Outliers identified:", length(outlier), "\n")
      cat("Proportion (%) of outliers:", 
          round(length(outlier) / sum(!is.na(var_name)) * 100, 1), "\n")
      cat("Mean of the outliers:", round(mo, 2), "\n")
      cat("Mean with outliers:", round(m1, 2), "\n")
    }
  }
  
  # Combine all outlier information into one data frame
  if (length(outlier_results) > 0) {
    combined_outliers <- do.call(rbind, outlier_results)
  } else {
    combined_outliers <- data.frame()  # If no outliers were found, return an empty data frame
  }
  
  # Return combined data frame with outliers information
  return(combined_outliers)
}



# Call the function and store the results
outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate <- outlierKD_feeding_rate(afdw_feeding_rate, id_col = "treatment", start_col = 6, end_col = 6)

Outliers for column: feeding_rate 
Outliers identified: 38 
Proportion (%) of outliers: 11.5 
Mean of the outliers: 14.87 
Mean with outliers: 3.05 
# Print the combined outlier information
print(outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate)
                      Column Row Replicate   Outlier
feeding_rate.1  feeding_rate  23        NC 45.410863
feeding_rate.2  feeding_rate  26        NC 31.850622
feeding_rate.3  feeding_rate  28        NC  9.967544
feeding_rate.4  feeding_rate  33       Pro 12.372582
feeding_rate.5  feeding_rate  34       Pro 23.202522
feeding_rate.6  feeding_rate  35       Pro 16.320830
feeding_rate.7  feeding_rate  36       Pro 26.784835
feeding_rate.8  feeding_rate  37       Pro 11.390999
feeding_rate.9  feeding_rate  38       Pro 15.479254
feeding_rate.10 feeding_rate  39       Pro 17.581940
feeding_rate.11 feeding_rate  40       Pro 23.552156
feeding_rate.12 feeding_rate  42       Pro 25.109351
feeding_rate.13 feeding_rate  43       Pro 13.411997
feeding_rate.14 feeding_rate  44       Pro 15.941264
feeding_rate.15 feeding_rate  45       Pro 13.484484
feeding_rate.16 feeding_rate  47       Pro 18.715968
feeding_rate.17 feeding_rate  48       Pro  9.594064
feeding_rate.18 feeding_rate  60       Pro  9.878984
feeding_rate.19 feeding_rate  91       Mix  8.922841
feeding_rate.20 feeding_rate  93       Mix 10.750982
feeding_rate.21 feeding_rate 116       Mix 15.953187
feeding_rate.22 feeding_rate 118       Mix  7.739009
feeding_rate.23 feeding_rate 125        NC 11.963002
feeding_rate.24 feeding_rate 131        NC  7.543135
feeding_rate.25 feeding_rate 132        NC  8.173696
feeding_rate.26 feeding_rate 135        NC  6.728001
feeding_rate.27 feeding_rate 142        NC 11.356729
feeding_rate.28 feeding_rate 144        NC 13.312560
feeding_rate.29 feeding_rate 182       Cip  8.006604
feeding_rate.30 feeding_rate 190       Cip 10.259977
feeding_rate.31 feeding_rate 247        NC  7.966650
feeding_rate.32 feeding_rate 271       Pro 23.456943
feeding_rate.33 feeding_rate 277       Pro  6.978045
feeding_rate.34 feeding_rate 280       Pro 10.906601
feeding_rate.35 feeding_rate 284       Pro 13.300408
feeding_rate.36 feeding_rate 289       Pro 22.166337
feeding_rate.37 feeding_rate 293       Pro  8.206225
feeding_rate.38 feeding_rate 313       Cip 11.233729
# Assuming outlier_info_combined is the combined outlier data frame
outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate <- outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate  # If not already assigned

# Count the number of outliers for each replicate
outlier_counts_feeding_rate <- table(outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate$Replicate)

# Convert to a data frame for better readability
outlier_counts_feeding_rate_df <- as.data.frame(outlier_counts_feeding_rate)
colnames(outlier_counts_feeding_rate_df) <- c("Replicate", "Outlier_Count")

View(outlier_counts_feeding_rate_df)

Remove outliers:

# Extract the row numbers of the outliers
outlier_rows_feeding_rate <- outlier_info_combined_feeding_rate$Row

# Remove the outlier rows from the dataset
afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 <- afdw_feeding_rate[-outlier_rows_feeding_rate, ]

Check histogram again:

hist(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ feeding_rate) 

skewness(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ feeding_rate, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 1.352644
shapiro.test(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ feeding_rate)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$feeding_rate
W = 0.87441, p-value = 1.013e-14

Still not normal distributed and right skewed, but skewness now 1.3, therefore no data transformation for later model.

Calculate mean feeding rate for each week

mean_feeding_rate <- afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 %>%
  group_by(week, treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_feeding_rate = mean(feeding_rate, na.rm = TRUE))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'week'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(mean_feeding_rate)
# A tibble: 12 × 3
# Groups:   week [3]
    week treatment mean_feeding_rate
   <int> <chr>                 <dbl>
 1     1 Cip                   1.25 
 2     1 Mix                   1.21 
 3     1 NC                    1.66 
 4     1 Pro                   2.56 
 5     2 Cip                   1.54 
 6     2 Mix                   0.841
 7     2 NC                    2.37 
 8     2 Pro                   1.35 
 9     3 Cip                   2.08 
10     3 Mix                   1.55 
11     3 NC                    1.35 
12     3 Pro                   1.20

Now calculate mean feeding for each treatment:

mean_feeding_rate_treatment <- afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_feeding_rate = mean(feeding_rate, na.rm = TRUE))

print(mean_feeding_rate_treatment)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_feeding_rate
  <chr>                 <dbl>
1 Cip                    1.62
2 Mix                    1.20
3 NC                     1.76
4 Pro                    1.54

Model feeding rate

# week, treatment and beaker as factor
afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ week <- factor(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ week)
afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ treatment <- factor(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ treatment)
afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ beaker <- factor(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1 $ beaker)

# response variable: feeding_rate
# fixed effects: week + treatment + week:treatment
# random effect with constant intercept: (1|beaker)
# first check for NAs
any(is.na(afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1$feeding_rate))
[1] TRUE
feeding_rate_lmer <- lmer(feeding_rate ~ week + treatment + week:treatment + (1|beaker), data = afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1)

par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(feeding_rate_lmer) # point cloud looks evenly distributed --> normal distribution

qreference(residuals(feeding_rate_lmer)) # normality ok

drop1(feeding_rate_lmer, test = 'Chisq')
Single term deletions

Model:
feeding_rate ~ week + treatment + week:treatment + (1 | beaker)
               npar    AIC    LRT   Pr(Chi)    
<none>              1000.2                     
week:treatment    6 1015.5 27.363 0.0001238 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# interaction of timepoint (week) and treatment highly significant
# now check at which timepoints is a difference between treatments
anova(feeding_rate_lmer)
Analysis of Variance Table
               npar Sum Sq Mean Sq F value
week              2  0.390  0.1952  0.1232
treatment         3 11.210  3.7367  2.3592
week:treatment    6 44.774  7.4624  4.7115
# conduct Tukey HSD-test for pairwise comparisons
feeding_rate_tukey <- lsmeans(feeding_rate_lmer, ~ treatment | week)
feeding_rate_tukey
week = 1:
 treatment lsmean    SE  df lower.CL upper.CL
 Cip        1.254 0.262 277    0.739     1.77
 Mix        1.211 0.257 279    0.706     1.72
 NC         1.661 0.262 278    1.146     2.18
 Pro        2.556 0.378 280    1.812     3.30

week = 2:
 treatment lsmean    SE  df lower.CL upper.CL
 Cip        1.551 0.273 278    1.014     2.09
 Mix        0.841 0.239 277    0.370     1.31
 NC         2.385 0.279 279    1.836     2.93
 Pro        1.345 0.252 277    0.850     1.84

week = 3:
 treatment lsmean    SE  df lower.CL upper.CL
 Cip        2.092 0.267 278    1.566     2.62
 Mix        1.552 0.239 277    1.081     2.02
 NC         1.353 0.252 278    0.857     1.85
 Pro        1.185 0.286 279    0.622     1.75

Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
Confidence level used: 0.95 
feeding_rate_tukey1 <- contrast(feeding_rate_tukey, 'pairwise')
feeding_rate_tukey1
week = 1:
 contrast  estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix   0.0431 0.367 278   0.118  0.9994
 Cip - NC   -0.4070 0.370 278  -1.099  0.6903
 Cip - Pro  -1.3018 0.460 280  -2.830  0.0256
 Mix - NC   -0.4501 0.367 278  -1.227  0.6100
 Mix - Pro  -1.3449 0.457 280  -2.942  0.0185

week = 2:
 contrast  estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix   0.7102 0.363 278   1.958  0.2067
 Cip - NC   -0.8341 0.390 279  -2.136  0.1442
 Cip - Pro   0.2055 0.371 278   0.553  0.9456
 Mix - NC   -1.5443 0.367 278  -4.203  0.0002
 Mix - Pro  -0.5047 0.347 277  -1.454  0.4670
 NC - Pro    1.0396 0.376 278   2.765  0.0307

week = 3:
 contrast  estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix   0.5407 0.358 278   1.508  0.4338
 Cip - NC    0.7398 0.367 278   2.015  0.1851
 Cip - Pro   0.9077 0.391 278   2.320  0.0959
 Mix - NC    0.1991 0.347 277   0.573  0.9400
 Mix - Pro   0.3670 0.372 278   0.985  0.7580
 NC - Pro    0.1679 0.381 278   0.441  0.9713

Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 4 estimates 
feeding_rate_summary <- summary(feeding_rate_tukey1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt')
feeding_rate_summary
week = 1:
 contrast  estimate    SE  df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix   0.0431 0.367 278  -0.9022    0.988   0.118  0.9994
 Cip - NC   -0.4070 0.370 278  -1.3615    0.548  -1.099  0.6877
 Cip - Pro  -1.3018 0.460 280  -2.4882   -0.115  -2.830  0.0251
 Mix - NC   -0.4501 0.367 278  -1.3952    0.495  -1.227  0.6071
 Mix - Pro  -1.3449 0.457 280  -2.5234   -0.166  -2.942  0.0182
 NC - Pro   -0.8948 0.460 280  -2.0818    0.292  -1.945  0.2092

week = 2:
 contrast  estimate    SE  df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix   0.7102 0.363 278  -0.2280    1.648   1.958  0.2063

Confidence level used: 0.95

Differences: week 1: Cip-Pro and Mix_pro week 2: Mix-NC and NC-Pro week 3: none

feeding_rate_inter1 <- lsmeans(feeding_rate_lmer, ~ week |  treatment)

feeding_rate_inter_dunn1 <- contrast(feeding_rate_inter1, 'pairwise')

(feeding_rate_inter_dunn_sum1 <- summary(feeding_rate_inter_dunn1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt'))
treatment = Cip:
 contrast      estimate    SE  df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2   -0.296 0.364 182   -1.157   0.5643  -0.814  0.6949
 week1 - week3   -0.838 0.360 180   -1.689   0.0129  -2.328  0.0546
 week2 - week3   -0.541 0.368 185   -1.411   0.3285  -1.470  0.3075

treatment = Mix:
 contrast      estimate    SE  df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2    0.371 0.338 186   -0.428   1.1696   1.096  0.5179
 week1 - week3   -0.340 0.338 186   -1.139   0.4587  -1.006  0.5738
 week2 - week3   -0.711 0.325 177   -1.479   0.0571  -2.188  0.0760

treatment = NC:
 contrast      estimate    SE  df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2   -0.724 0.371 201   -1.599   0.1522  -1.951  0.1272
 week1 - week3    0.309 0.350 186   -0.519   1.1367   0.881  0.6527
 week2 - week3    1.032 0.364 195    0.174   1.8912   2.839  0.0138

treatment = Pro:
 contrast      estimate    SE  df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 week1 - week2    1.211 0.444 225    0.166   2.2556   2.728  0.0185
 week1 - week3    1.372 0.464 234    0.279   2.4642   2.954  0.0095
 week2 - week3    0.161 0.368 193   -0.707   1.0285   0.437  0.8995

Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 3 estimates 
P value adjustment: mvt method for 3 tests

Weeks for all treatments similar, only difference for treatment NC: week 2 & 3 and for treatment Pro: week 1 & 3

Boxplot feeding rate after 7/14/21 days

feeding_rate_boxplot <- ggplot(data = afdw_feeding_rate_cleaned_1, 
       mapping = aes(x = factor(week), 
                     y = feeding_rate, 
                     fill = factor(treatment, levels = c("NC", "Cip", "Pro", "Mix")))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) + 
  scale_color_okabe_ito() + 
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = NULL , fill = "Treatment") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Week 1", "Week 2", "Week 3")) +
  labs(y = expression("Feeding rate (µg/day)"))

feeding_rate_boxplot
Warning: Removed 30 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_boxplot()`).

# save the plot
ggsave(feeding_rate_boxplot, 
       filename = "feeding_rate_boxplot.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image
Warning: Removed 30 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_boxplot()`).

4.2 AFDW after feeding

Calculate mean of afdw after feeding for each week

mean_afdw_after_feeding <- afdw_feeding_rate %>%
  group_by(week, treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_afdw_after_feeding = mean(afdw))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'week'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(mean_afdw_after_feeding)
# A tibble: 12 × 3
# Groups:   week [3]
    week treatment mean_afdw_after_feeding
   <int> <chr>                       <dbl>
 1     1 Cip                        NA    
 2     1 Mix                         0.129
 3     1 NC                         NA    
 4     1 Pro                        NA    
 5     2 Cip                        NA    
 6     2 Mix                         0.130
 7     2 NC                         NA    
 8     2 Pro                        NA    
 9     3 Cip                        NA    
10     3 Mix                         0.292
11     3 NC                         NA    
12     3 Pro                        NA

Calculate mean of afdw after feeding for each treatment

mean_afdw_after_feeding_treatment <- afdw_feeding_rate %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_afdw_after_feeding = mean(afdw))

print(mean_afdw_after_feeding_treatment)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_afdw_after_feeding
  <chr>                       <dbl>
1 Cip                        NA    
2 Mix                         0.184
3 NC                         NA    
4 Pro                        NA

Histogram afdw after feeding

hist(afdw_feeding_rate $ afdw) 

skewness(afdw_feeding_rate $ afdw, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 1.052855
shapiro.test(afdw_feeding_rate $ afdw)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  afdw_feeding_rate$afdw
W = 0.90351, p-value = 1.164e-13

AFDW after feeding not normal distributed, skewness = 1.05, no data transformation.

Model afdw after feeding

# response variable: afdw
# fixed effects: week + treatment + week:treatment
# random effect with constant intercept: (1|beaker)
# remove NAs
afdw_feeding_rate <- na.omit(afdw_feeding_rate)

afdw_after_feeding_lmer <- lmer(afdw ~ week + treatment + week:treatment + (1|beaker), data = afdw_feeding_rate)

#----- new try without random effect
model_afdw2 <- lm(afdw ~ week + treatment + week:treatment, data = afdw_feeding_rate)

drop1(model_afdw2, test = "Chisq")
Single term deletions

Model:
afdw ~ week + treatment + week:treatment
               Df Sum of Sq    RSS     AIC  Pr(>Chi)    
<none>                      3.3639 -1497.4              
week:treatment  3   0.38412 3.7481 -1467.7 8.746e-08 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(model_afdw2)
Analysis of Variance Table

Response: afdw
                Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value    Pr(>F)    
week             1 4.0166  4.0166 384.472 < 2.2e-16 ***
treatment        3 0.7822  0.2607  24.958 1.520e-14 ***
week:treatment   3 0.3841  0.1280  12.256 1.298e-07 ***
Residuals      322 3.3639  0.0104                      
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
#----------
plot(afdw_after_feeding_lmer) # point cloud looks evenly distributed --> normal distribution

qreference(residuals(afdw_after_feeding_lmer)) # normality

drop1(afdw_after_feeding_lmer, test = 'Chisq')
Single term deletions

Model:
afdw ~ week + treatment + week:treatment + (1 | beaker)
               npar     AIC    LRT   Pr(Chi)    
<none>              -558.27                     
week:treatment    3 -527.21 37.054 4.482e-08 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# interaction of timepoint (week) and treatment highly significant
# now check at which timepoints is a difference between treatments
anova(afdw_after_feeding_lmer)
Analysis of Variance Table
               npar Sum Sq Mean Sq F value
week              1 4.0166  4.0166 414.332
treatment         3 0.6329  0.2110  21.762
week:treatment    3 0.3841  0.1280  13.208
# conduct Tukey HSD-test for pairwise comparisons
afdw_tukey <- lsmeans(afdw_after_feeding_lmer, ~ treatment | week)
afdw_tukey
week = 2:
 treatment lsmean     SE  df lower.CL upper.CL
 Cip        0.280 0.0126 106    0.255    0.305
 Mix        0.184 0.0115 106    0.161    0.206
 NC         0.285 0.0119 106    0.262    0.309
 Pro        0.306 0.0122 106    0.282    0.330

Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
Confidence level used: 0.95 
afdw_tukey1 <- contrast(afdw_tukey, 'pairwise')
afdw_tukey
week = 2:
 treatment lsmean     SE  df lower.CL upper.CL
 Cip        0.280 0.0126 106    0.255    0.305
 Mix        0.184 0.0115 106    0.161    0.206
 NC         0.285 0.0119 106    0.262    0.309
 Pro        0.306 0.0122 106    0.282    0.330

Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
Confidence level used: 0.95 
afdw_summary <- summary(afdw_tukey1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt')
afdw_summary
week = 2:
 contrast  estimate     SE  df lower.CL upper.CL t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix  0.09657 0.0171 106   0.0519   0.1412   5.643  <.0001
 Cip - NC  -0.00521 0.0174 106  -0.0506   0.0402  -0.299  0.9906
 Cip - Pro -0.02606 0.0175 106  -0.0718   0.0197  -1.486  0.4497
 Mix - NC  -0.10178 0.0166 106  -0.1451  -0.0584  -6.129  <.0001
 Mix - Pro -0.12263 0.0168 106  -0.1664  -0.0789  -7.315  <.0001
 NC - Pro  -0.02085 0.0170 106  -0.0653   0.0236  -1.223  0.6134

Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
Confidence level used: 0.95 
Conf-level adjustment: mvt method for 6 estimates 
P value adjustment: mvt method for 6 tests

Here treatments quite different within weeks. Compare weeks:

afdw_after_feeding_inter1 <- lsmeans(afdw_after_feeding_lmer, ~ week |  treatment)

afdw_after_feeding_dunn1 <- contrast(afdw_after_feeding_inter1, 'pairwise')

(afdw_after_feeding_dunn_sum1 <- summary(afdw_after_feeding_dunn1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt'))
Warning in summary.emmGrid(afdw_after_feeding_dunn1, infer = TRUE, adjust =
"mvt"): 'mvt' adjustment not available due to missing values
treatment = Cip:
 contrast  estimate SE df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL z.ratio p.value
 (nothing)   nonEst NA NA        NA        NA      NA      NA

treatment = Mix:
 contrast  estimate SE df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL z.ratio p.value
 (nothing)   nonEst NA NA        NA        NA      NA      NA

treatment = NC:
 contrast  estimate SE df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL z.ratio p.value
 (nothing)   nonEst NA NA        NA        NA      NA      NA

treatment = Pro:
 contrast  estimate SE df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL z.ratio p.value
 (nothing)   nonEst NA NA        NA        NA      NA      NA

Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
Confidence level used: 0.95

Week 1 & 2 for treatments Mix & Pro similar. Everything else shows differences.

Boxplot afdw after feeding of 7/14/21 days

afdw_after_feeding_boxplot <- ggplot(data = afdw_feeding_rate, 
       mapping = aes(x = factor(week), 
                     y = afdw, 
                     fill = factor(treatment, levels = c("NC", "Cip", "Pro", "Mix")))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) + 
  scale_color_okabe_ito() + 
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = NULL , fill = "Treatment") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Week 1", "Week 2", "Week 3")) +
  labs(y = expression("AFDW (mg/cm"^"2"*")"))

afdw_after_feeding_boxplot

# save the plot
ggsave(afdw_after_feeding_boxplot, 
       filename = "afdw_after_feeding_boxplot.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image

5 Dryweight and growth snails

Unit of dry weight = mg

# insert data (Culture snails from in-house breeding)
dryweight_snails <-
  read.table(file.path(prj, "data\\dryweight_snails.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(dryweight_snails)
# 3 snails from the 20 baseline (culture) snails were lost during the analyses.

Calculate mean dryweight of all treatments and the culture snails

mean_dryweight_snails <- dryweight_snails %>%
              group_by(treatment) %>%
              summarize(mean_dryweight_snails = mean(dryweight_mg))

print(mean_dryweight_snails)
# A tibble: 5 × 2
  treatment mean_dryweight_snails
  <chr>                     <dbl>
1 Cip                       0.534
2 Culture                   0.439
3 Mix                       0.507
4 NC                        0.679
5 Pro                       0.553

Snails from feeding assay (fed with biofilm) heavier than snails from culture (fed with Tetraphyll flakes).

Calculate growth:

growth_snails <- dryweight_snails %>%
  dplyr::mutate(growth_µg_dw_per_day = ((dryweight_mg - median(dryweight_mg[treatment == "Culture"])) / 7) * 1000) 
# multiply by 1000 to obtain a growth unit of µg dryweight per day

# calculate the growth in percentage in comparison to culture snails
percental_growth <- growth_snails %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_dryweight = mean(dryweight_mg)) %>%
  mutate(percental_growth = (mean_dryweight / mean_dryweight[treatment == "Culture"]) * 100 - 100)

percental_growth
# A tibble: 5 × 3
  treatment mean_dryweight percental_growth
  <chr>              <dbl>            <dbl>
1 Cip                0.534             21.8
2 Culture            0.439              0  
3 Mix                0.507             15.7
4 NC                 0.679             54.8
5 Pro                0.553             26.1
# calculate effects size compared to control snails
effect_size_growth <- growth_snails %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_dryweight = mean(dryweight_mg)) %>%
  mutate(effect_size = (mean_dryweight / mean_dryweight[treatment == "NC"]) * 100 - 100)

effect_size_growth
# A tibble: 5 × 3
  treatment mean_dryweight effect_size
  <chr>              <dbl>       <dbl>
1 Cip                0.534       -21.3
2 Culture            0.439       -35.4
3 Mix                0.507       -25.3
4 NC                 0.679         0  
5 Pro                0.553       -18.5

Histogram snail growth

# filter culture snails
filtered_growth <- growth_snails %>%
  filter(!treatment %in% c("Culture"))

hist(filtered_growth $ growth_µg_dw_per_day) 

skewness(filtered_growth $ growth_µg_dw_per_day, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 0.5211954
shapiro.test(filtered_growth $ growth_µg_dw_per_day)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  filtered_growth$growth_µg_dw_per_day
W = 0.9753, p-value = 0.52

Growth normal distributed.

Model growth snails

# explanatory variable treatment as factor
filtered_growth $ treatment <- factor(filtered_growth $ treatment)
  
  
model_growth_snails <- lm(growth_µg_dw_per_day ~ treatment, data = filtered_growth)

plot(model_growth_snails) # point cloud looks evenly distributed --> normal distribution

qreference(residuals(model_growth_snails)) # normal distribution

anova(model_growth_snails)
Analysis of Variance Table

Response: growth_µg_dw_per_day
          Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value   Pr(>F)   
treatment  3 3534.2 1178.05  5.0763 0.004928 **
Residuals 36 8354.5  232.07                    
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Explanatory variable “treatment” highly significant. Now check differences between treatments:

growth_snails_inter <- lsmeans(model_growth_snails, ~ treatment)

growth_snails_inter_dunn1 <- contrast(growth_snails_inter, 'pairwise')

growth_snails_inter_dunn_sum1 <- summary(growth_snails_inter_dunn1, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'mvt')

growth_snails_inter_dunn1
 contrast  estimate   SE df t.ratio p.value
 Cip - Mix     3.84 6.81 36   0.564  0.9422
 Cip - NC    -20.65 6.81 36  -3.031  0.0223
 Cip - Pro    -2.66 6.81 36  -0.391  0.9794
 Mix - NC    -24.49 6.81 36  -3.595  0.0051
 Mix - Pro    -6.50 6.81 36  -0.955  0.7757
 NC - Pro     17.99 6.81 36   2.640  0.0564

P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 4 estimates
  • Significant difference between growth of Cip and Mix snails compared to NC; almost significant difference (p=0.0564) between Pro treatment and NC

Boxplot growth snails

# plot
growth_snails_boxplot <- ggplot(data = filtered_growth, 
                                mapping = aes(x = factor(treatment, levels = c("NC", "Cip", "Pro", "Mix")), 
                                              y = growth_µg_dw_per_day, 
                                              fill = factor(treatment, levels = c("NC", "Cip", "Pro", "Mix")))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Mix" = "#F0E442"),
                    breaks = c("NC", "Cip", "Pro", "Mix")) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_okabe_ito() + 
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = NULL , y = "Growth (µg dryweight / day)", fill = "Treatment") +
  ylim(-10, 90) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("NC", "Cip", "Pro", "Mix")) +
  labs(title = expression(paste("Growth of ", italic("Potamopyrgus antipodarum"), " after 21-day feeding assay"))) +
  annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = max(filtered_growth$growth_µg_dw_per_day) + 5, 
           label = "p=0.022*", size = 3) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 1, xend = 2, y = max(growth_µg_dw_per_day) + 2, yend = max(growth_µg_dw_per_day) + 2),
               lineend = "round", linewidth = 0.7) +
  annotate("text", x = 2.5, y = max(filtered_growth$growth_µg_dw_per_day) + 21, 
           label = "p=0.005**", size = 3) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 1, xend = 4, y = max(growth_µg_dw_per_day) + 18, yend = max(growth_µg_dw_per_day) + 18),
               lineend = "round", linewidth = 0.7) +
    annotate("text", x = 2, y = max(filtered_growth$growth_µg_dw_per_day) + 13, 
           label = "p=0.056", size = 3) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = 1, xend = 3, y = max(growth_µg_dw_per_day) + 10, yend = max(growth_µg_dw_per_day) + 10),
               lineend = "round", linewidth = 0.7) 


growth_snails_boxplot
Warning: Removed 1 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_boxplot()`).
Warning: Removed 1 rows containing missing values (`geom_point()`).

# save the plot
ggsave(growth_snails_boxplot, 
       filename = "growth_snails_boxplot.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image
Warning: Removed 1 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_boxplot()`).
Removed 1 rows containing missing values (`geom_point()`).

6 Fatty acid composition snails

Statistics for fatty acid composition of snails in absolute values (µg fatty acid / mg dry weight snail).

# insert data
FA_Snails_absolute <-
  read.table(file.path(prj, "data\\FA_Snails_absolute.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(FA_Snails_absolute)

any(is.na(FA_Snails_absolute))
[1] FALSE

Following fatty acids were not considered for FA content in snails due to too less values (less than 3 findings): C10:0, C11:0, C14:1, C15:1, C17:1, C20:0 and C22:0.

Therefore, delete rows with respective fatty acids:

FA_Snails_absolute <- FA_Snails_absolute %>%
  filter(!fa %in% c("C10:0", "C11:0", "C14:1", "C15:1", "C17:1", "C20:0", "C22:0"))

Total SAFA, MUFA & PUFA content

1) Total SAFA content

# total SAFA, MUFA, PUFA content; sa = "snail absolute" 
# total SAFA content 
# filter saturated fatty acids in new table 
SAFA_sa <- FA_Snails_absolute %>%
filter(fa %in% c("C08:0", "C12:0", "C13:0", "C14:0", "C15:0", "C16:0", "C17:0", "C18:0", "C21:0", "C24:0")) 

# calculate the total SAFA content per snail 
total_SAFA_per_snail <- SAFA_sa %>% 
  group_by(replicate, treatment) %>% 
  summarize(sum_SAFA = sum(fa_conc)) 
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'replicate'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(total_SAFA_per_snail) 
# A tibble: 40 × 3
# Groups:   replicate [40]
   replicate treatment sum_SAFA
   <chr>     <chr>        <dbl>
 1 S_1       NC            759.
 2 S_10      NC            238.
 3 S_11      Pro           398.
 4 S_12      Pro           339.
 5 S_13      Pro           400.
 6 S_14      Pro           293.
 7 S_15      Pro           187.
 8 S_16      Pro           212.
 9 S_17      Pro           270.
10 S_18      Pro           272.
# ℹ 30 more rows
# mean 
mean_SAFA_sa <- total_SAFA_per_snail %>% 
  group_by(treatment) %>% 
  summarize(mean_SAFA = mean(sum_SAFA, na.rm = TRUE)) 
print(mean_SAFA_sa)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_SAFA
  <chr>         <dbl>
1 Cip            457.
2 Mix            358.
3 NC             405.
4 Pro            309.

2) Total MUFA content

# filter mono-unsaturated fatty acids in new table
MUFA_sa <- FA_Snails_absolute %>%
  filter(fa %in% c("C16:1", "C18:1", "C20:1", "C22:1", "C24:1"))

# total MUFA content per snail
total_MUFA_per_snail <- MUFA_sa %>%
  group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  summarize(sum_MUFA = sum(fa_conc))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'replicate'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(total_MUFA_per_snail)
# A tibble: 40 × 3
# Groups:   replicate [40]
   replicate treatment sum_MUFA
   <chr>     <chr>        <dbl>
 1 S_1       NC            670.
 2 S_10      NC            253.
 3 S_11      Pro           473.
 4 S_12      Pro           373.
 5 S_13      Pro           403.
 6 S_14      Pro           222.
 7 S_15      Pro           263.
 8 S_16      Pro           149.
 9 S_17      Pro           175.
10 S_18      Pro           342.
# ℹ 30 more rows
# mean
mean_MUFA_sa <- total_MUFA_per_snail %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_MUFA = mean(sum_MUFA, na.rm = TRUE))
print(mean_MUFA_sa)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_MUFA
  <chr>         <dbl>
1 Cip            370.
2 Mix            332.
3 NC             415.
4 Pro            328.

3) Total PUFA content

# filter poly-unsaturated fatty acids in new table
PUFA_sa <- FA_Snails_absolute %>%
  filter(fa %in% c("C18:2(n-6c)", "C18:2(n-6t)", "C18:3(n-3)", "C18:3(n-6)", "C18:4(n-3)", "C20:2(n-6)", "C20:3(n-6)", "C20:4(n-6)", "C20:5(n-3)", "C22:2", "C22:5(n-3)", "C22:6(n-3)"))
  
# calculate total PUFA content per snail
total_PUFA_per_snail <- PUFA_sa %>%
  group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  summarize(sum_PUFA = sum(fa_conc))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'replicate'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(total_PUFA_per_snail)

   replicate treatment sum_PUFA

 8 S_16      Pro          149. 
 9 S_17      Pro           93.6
10 S_18      Pro          111. 
# ℹ 30 more rows
# mean
mean_PUFA_sa <- total_PUFA_per_snail %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_PUFA = mean(sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE))
print(mean_PUFA_sa)

  <chr>         <dbl>
1 Cip            152.
2 Mix            157.
3 NC             231.
4 Pro            195.

4) Total FA per snail

# total FA 
total_FA_snails <- bind_rows(total_SAFA_per_snail, total_MUFA_per_snail, total_PUFA_per_snail)

total_FA_per_snail <- total_FA_snails %>%
  group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  summarize(sum_FA = sum(sum_SAFA, sum_MUFA, sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'replicate'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(total_FA_per_snail)

# mean
mean_total_FA_sa <- total_FA_per_snail %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_FA = mean(sum_FA, na.rm = TRUE))
print(mean_total_FA_sa)

Histogram FA snails

hist(FA_Snails_absolute $ fa_conc) 

skewness(FA_Snails_absolute $ fa_conc, na.rm = TRUE)

data:  FA_Snails_absolute$fa_conc

Fatty acid concentration not normal distributed and right skewed.

NMDS FA snails

Change table to wide format:

# change table to wide format (sqrt for square root transformation in next step)
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <-
  pivot_wider(FA_Snails_absolute,
              names_from = fa,
              values_from = fa_conc)

# square root transformation, to reduce discriminatory power of dominant fa groups
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt[, 3:28] <- sqrt(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt[, 3:28])

any(is.na(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt))
[1] FALSE

Check for outlier:

#Run function one time, after that you can use the shortcut outlierKD (your subset, variable)
outlierKD_multiple <- function(dt, id_col = "replicate", start_col = 3, end_col = 28) {
  # Initialize an empty list to store results for each column
  outlier_results <- list()
  
  # Loop over each column from 3 to 28 (or specify your range)
  for (i in start_col:end_col) {
    col_name <- colnames(dt)[i]  # Get the column name
    
    # Extract the column values
    var_name <- dt[[col_name]]
    
    # Check if the column is numeric
    if (is.numeric(var_name)) {
      na1 <- sum(is.na(var_name))
      m1 <- mean(var_name, na.rm = TRUE)
      
      # Identify outliers using boxplot stats
      outlier <- boxplot.stats(var_name)$out  
      
      # Find rows with outliers
      outlier_rows <- which(var_name %in% outlier)
      
      # If no outliers, skip the rest of the loop
      if (length(outlier_rows) == 0) next
      
      # Get replicate values for the outliers
      id_values <- dt[[id_col]][outlier_rows]
      
      # Mean of outliers
      mo <- mean(outlier)
      
      # Collect the outlier information for this column
      outlier_results[[col_name]] <- data.frame(
        Column = col_name,
        Row = outlier_rows,
        Replicate = id_values,
        Outlier = outlier
      )
      
      cat("\nOutliers for column:", col_name, "\n")
      cat("Outliers identified:", length(outlier), "\n")
      cat("Proportion (%) of outliers:", 
          round(length(outlier) / sum(!is.na(var_name)) * 100, 1), "\n")
      cat("Mean of the outliers:", round(mo, 2), "\n")
      cat("Mean with outliers:", round(m1, 2), "\n")
    }
  }
  
  # Combine all outlier information into one data frame
  if (length(outlier_results) > 0) {
    combined_outliers <- do.call(rbind, outlier_results)
  } else {
    combined_outliers <- data.frame()  # If no outliers were found, return an empty data frame
  }
  
  # Return combined data frame with outliers information
  return(combined_outliers)
}



# Call the function and store the results
outlier_info_combined <- outlierKD_multiple(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt, id_col = "replicate", start_col = 3, end_col = 28)

outlier_info_combined <- outlier_info_combined  

# Count the number of outliers for each replicate
outlier_counts <- table(outlier_info_combined$Replicate)

# Convert to a data frame for better readability
outlier_counts_df <- as.data.frame(outlier_counts)
colnames(outlier_counts_df) <- c("Replicate", "Outlier_Count")

# Sort the results by the number of outliers (descending)
outlier_counts_df_sorted <- outlier_counts_df[order(-outlier_counts_df$Outlier_Count), ]


print(outlier_counts_df_sorted)
   Replicate Outlier_Count
1        S_1            13
2       S_13            12
6       S_25            12
9       S_30            11
5       S_19             3
8       S_29             2
3       S_14             1
4       S_17             1
7       S_28             1
10      S_37             1
11      S_39             1

As the replicates S_1, S_13, S_25 and S_30 show more than 10 outliers within 26 fatty acids concentrations, these replicates will be removed.

FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <- FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt %>%
  filter(!(replicate %in% c("S_1", "S_13", "S_25", "S_30")))

Perform metaMDS:

# Remove first and second column for metaMDS
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <- FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt[, -c(1, 2)]

# Rename (- C, - () ) fatty acids for later plot
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <- FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt %>%
  rename(
    `8:0`      = `C08:0`,   
    `12:0`     = `C12:0`,   
    `13:0`     = `C13:0`,
    `14:0`     = `C14:0`,
    `15:0`     = `C15:0`,
    `16:0`     = `C16:0`,
    `16:1`     = `C16:1`,
    `18:0`     = `C18:0`,
    `18:1`     = `C18:1`,
    `18:2n-6t` = `C18:2(n-6t)`,
    `18:2n-6c` = `C18:2(n-6c)`,
    `18:3n-6`  = `C18:3(n-6)`,
    `18:3n-3`  = `C18:3(n-3)`,
    `18:4n-3`  = `C18:4(n-3)`,
    `20:1`     = `C20:1`,
    `20:2n-6`  = `C20:2(n-6)`,
    `20:3n-6`  = `C20:3(n-6)`,
    `20:4n-6`  = `C20:4(n-6)`,
    `21:0`     = `C21:0`,
    `20:5n-3`  = `C20:5(n-3)`,
    `22:1`     = `C22:1`,
    `22:2`     = `C22:2`,
    `22:5n-3`  = `C22:5(n-3)`,
    `22:6n-3`  = `C22:6(n-3)`,
    `24:0`     = `C24:0`,
    `24:1`     = `C24:1`
  )



NMDS_Snails_absolute <-
  metaMDS(
    FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
    autotransform = FALSE,
    k = 2 ,
    distance = "bray",
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
Run 0 stress 0.1392313 
Run 1 stress 0.1819361 
Run 2 stress 0.1493108 
Run 3 stress 0.1710073 
Run 4 stress 0.2043156 
Run 5 stress 0.1623304 
Run 6 stress 0.1493108 
Run 7 stress 0.1493108 
Run 8 stress 0.1778741 
Run 9 stress 0.1808604 
Run 10 stress 0.1392313

... Procrustes: rmse 4.785395e-06  max resid 2.252332e-05 
... Similar to previous best
Run 11 stress 0.1392313 
... Procrustes: rmse 6.123803e-06  max resid 2.783414e-05 
... Similar to previous best
Run 12 stress 0.1493108 
Run 13 stress 0.1710073 
Run 14 stress 0.1493108 
Run 15 stress 0.1493108 
Run 16 stress 0.1392313 
... Procrustes: rmse 2.65966e-06  max resid 7.743661e-06 
... Similar to previous best
Run 17 stress 0.1835695 
Run 18 stress 0.1392313 
... Procrustes: rmse 2.30055e-06  max resid 1.195169e-05 
... Similar to previous best
Run 19 stress 0.1392313 
... Procrustes: rmse 4.266582e-06  max resid 1.675436e-05 
... Similar to previous best
Run 20 stress 0.1392313 
... Procrustes: rmse 1.910896e-06  max resid 7.819836e-06 
... Similar to previous best
*** Best solution repeated 6 times
NMDS_Snails_absolute$stress
[1] 0.1392313

Create fa vectors

# separation in SAFA; MUFA & PUFA to build vectors in NMDS
# "sa" = snail absolute
FA_variables.SAFAs_sa <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Snails_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "8:0",
        "12:0",
        "13:0",
        "14:0",
        "15:0",
        "16:0",
        "18:0",
        "21:0",
        "24:0"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

FA_variables.MUFAs_sa <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Snails_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "16:1",
        "18:1",
        "20:1",
        "22:1",
        "24:1"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

FA_variables.PUFAs_sa <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Snails_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "18:2n-6t",
        "18:2n-6c",
        "18:3n-6",
        "18:3n-3",
        "18:4n-3",
        "20:2n-6",
        "20:3n-6",
        "20:4n-6",
        "20:5n-3",
        "22:2",
        "22:5n-3",
        "22:6n-3"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

# Extract NMDS scores 
data.scores_snail_absolute = as.data.frame(scores(NMDS_Snails_absolute)$sites)

# Remove S_1, S_13, S_25 and S_30 from first data frame "FA_Snails_absolute"
FA_Snails_absolute <- FA_Snails_absolute %>%
  filter(!(replicate %in% c("S_1", "S_13", "S_25", "S_30")))

Treatments <-
  FA_Snails_absolute $ treatment[FA_Snails_absolute $ fa == "C16:0"]

# add treatment column to NMDS scores
data.scores_snail_absolute$Treatment = Treatments

# vectors for SAFA, MUFA and PUFA
SAFAs_coord_cont_sa = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.SAFAs_sa, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.SAFAs_sa)
MUFAs_coord_cont_sa = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.MUFAs_sa, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.MUFAs_sa)
PUFAs_coord_cont_sa = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.PUFAs_sa, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.PUFAs_sa)

Plot NMDS:

# For the plot: reorder treatment variable in data.scores_snail_absolute
data.scores_snail_absolute$Treatment <- factor(
  data.scores_snail_absolute$Treatment,
  levels = c("NC", "Cip", "Pro", "Mix")
)

# Compute convex hull for each Treatment
polygon_data_snails <- data.scores_snail_absolute %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(hull_points = list(data.frame(
    NMDS1 = NMDS1[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)],
    NMDS2 = NMDS2[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)]
  ))) %>%
  unnest(hull_points)

# plot
absol_snail_with_vectors <- ggplot(data = data.scores_snail_absolute, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
    geom_polygon(
    data = polygon_data_snails,
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = Treatment, fill = Treatment),
    alpha = 0.2,
    show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(shape = Treatment, color = Treatment),
    size = 5,
    alpha = 1,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("NC" = 16, "Pro" = 17, "Cip" = 18, "Mix" = 15)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) + 
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1 * 0.2, yend = NMDS2 * 0.2), 
               data = PUFAs_coord_cont_sa, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
    geom_segment(aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1 * 0.2, yend = NMDS2 * 0.2), 
               data = MUFAs_coord_cont_sa, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
      geom_segment(aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1 * 0.1, yend = NMDS2 * 0.1), 
               data = SAFAs_coord_cont_sa, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = PUFAs_coord_cont_sa, 
    aes(x = NMDS1 * 0.2, y = NMDS2 * 0.2, label = row.names(PUFAs_coord_cont_sa)), 
    colour = "black",
    size = 2.5) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = MUFAs_coord_cont_sa, 
    aes(x = NMDS1 * 0.2, y = NMDS2 * 0.2, label = row.names(MUFAs_coord_cont_sa)), 
    colour = "#996600",
    size = 2.5) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = SAFAs_coord_cont_sa, 
    aes(x = NMDS1 * 0.1, y = NMDS2 * 0.1, label = row.names(SAFAs_coord_cont_sa)), 
    colour = "#0000FF",
    size = 2.5) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 
Warning: The `size` argument of `element_line()` is deprecated as of ggplot2 3.4.0.
ℹ Please use the `linewidth` argument instead.
absol_snail_with_vectors
Warning: ggrepel: 3 unlabeled data points (too many overlaps). Consider
increasing max.overlaps

# position of FA names manually adjusted in vector graphic

Save the plot as .png:

ggsave(absol_snail_with_vectors, 
       filename = "NMDS_snails_absolute_vectors.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image
Warning: ggrepel: 3 unlabeled data points (too many overlaps). Consider
increasing max.overlaps

Save the plot as vector graphic:

ggsave(plot = absol_snail_with_vectors,
       "NMDS_snails_absolute_vectors.svg", 
       device = "svg",
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image
Warning: ggrepel: 3 unlabeled data points (too many overlaps). Consider
increasing max.overlaps

Now plot NMDS without fatty acid vectors:

# plot
absol_snail <- ggplot(data = data.scores_snail_absolute, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
    geom_polygon(
    data = polygon_data_snails,
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = Treatment, fill = Treatment),
    alpha = 0.2,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(shape = Treatment, color = Treatment),
    size = 5,
    alpha = 1,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("NC" = 16, "Pro" = 17, "Cip" = 18, "Mix" = 15)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) +  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 
Scale for colour is already present.
Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
absol_snail

Save the plot as .png:

ggsave(absol_snail, 
       filename = "NMDS_snails_absolute.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image

Save the plot as vector graphic:

ggsave(plot = absol_snail,
       "NMDS_snails_absolute.svg", 
       device = "svg",
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image

PERMANOVA FA snails

Create distance matrix with fatty acids:

FA_Snails_dist <- vegdist(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt, method = 'bray')

Separate explanatory variables from fatty acid information:

# change initial table to wide format again
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova <-
  pivot_wider(FA_Snails_absolute,
              names_from = fa,
              values_from = fa_conc)

# square root transformation again, to reduce discriminatory power of dominant fa groups
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova[, 3:28] <- sqrt(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova[, 3:28])

# delete fa columns
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova <- FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova[, c(1, 2)]
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova $ treatment <- as.factor(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova $ treatment)

Perform PERMANOVA:

perm_FA_Snails <- adonis2(FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment, data = FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
print(perm_FA_Snails)
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
Terms added sequentially (first to last)
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999

adonis2(formula = FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment, data = FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
treatment  3  0.18637 0.37538 6.4103  0.001 ***
Residual  32  0.31011 0.62462                  
Total     35  0.49648 1.00000                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Pairwise comparisons (by: Martinez Arbizu, P. (2020). pairwiseAdonis: Pairwise multilevel comparison using adonis. R package version 0.4):

pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis2(
  FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment,
  data = FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova,
  permutations = 999
)

# View the results
print(pairwise_results)
$parent_call
[1] "FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment , strata = Null , permutations 999"

$NC_vs_Pro
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)   
treatment  1 0.055214 0.23956 5.0405  0.005 **
Residual  16 0.175264 0.76044                 
Total     17 0.230478 1.00000                 
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

$NC_vs_Mix
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
treatment  1 0.088649 0.35016 8.0826  0.001 ***
Residual  15 0.164518 0.64984                  
Total     16 0.253168 1.00000                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

$NC_vs_Cip
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
treatment  1 0.030880 0.24787 5.6026  0.001 ***
Residual  17 0.093701 0.75213                  
Total     18 0.124581 1.00000                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

$Pro_vs_Mix
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
treatment  1 0.018204 0.07759 1.2618  0.253
Residual  15 0.216410 0.92241              
Total     16 0.234614 1.00000              

$Pro_vs_Cip
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
treatment  1 0.086188 0.37185 10.064  0.001 ***
Residual  17 0.145593 0.62815                  
Total     18 0.231780 1.00000                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

$Mix_vs_Cip
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
treatment  1  0.09303 0.40825 11.038  0.001 ***
Residual  16  0.13485 0.59175                  
Total     17  0.22788 1.00000                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

attr(,"class")
[1] "pwadstrata" "list"

All treatments except of Pro_vs_Mix significant differences.

SIMPER FA snails

# Upload extra table with treatments
snail_treatments_table <-
  read.table(file.path(prj, "data\\snail_treatments_table.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(snail_treatments_table)

# Perform SIMPER
simper_FA_Snails <- with(snail_treatments_table, simper(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt, treatment,  permutations = 999))

summary(simper_FA_Snails)
