 - attr(*, "class")= chr "simper"
 - attr(*, "permutations")= int 999
 - attr(*, "control")=List of 12
  ..$ within     :List of 6
  .. ..$ type    : chr "free"
  .. ..$ constant: logi FALSE
  .. ..$ mirror  : logi FALSE
  .. ..$ ncol    : NULL
  .. ..$ nrow    : NULL
  .. ..$ call    : language Within()
  .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "Within"
  ..$ plots      :List of 7
  .. ..$ strata    : NULL
  .. ..$ type      : chr "none"
  .. ..$ mirror    : logi FALSE
  .. ..$ ncol      : NULL
  .. ..$ nrow      : NULL
  .. ..$ plots.name: chr "NULL"
  .. ..$ call      : language Plots()
  .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "Plots"
  ..$ blocks     : NULL
  ..$ nperm      : num 999
  ..$ complete   : logi FALSE
  ..$ maxperm    : num 9999
  ..$ minperm    : num 5040
  ..$ all.perms  : NULL
  ..$ make       : logi TRUE
  ..$ observed   : logi FALSE
  ..$ blocks.name: chr "NULL"
  ..$ call       : language how(nperm = 999)
  ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "how"
# Combine results into a data frame
simper_results_snails <- do.call(rbind, lapply(simper_FA_Snails, function(x) {
  # Check if the element contains valid data
  if (!is.null(x$p)) {
    data.frame(
      fatty_acid = names(x$p),  
      p = as.numeric(x$p),      
      average = as.numeric(x$average),  
      sd = as.numeric(x$sd),
      ratio = as.numeric(x$ratio),
      cusum = as.numeric(x$cusum),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    NULL  # Skip if no p-values
  }
}))


# Show only significant results
simper_results_snails %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::select(fatty_acid, p, average, sd, ratio, cusum)  
           fatty_acid     p      average          sd     ratio     cusum

# How often do they occur
simper_results_snails %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::count(fatty_acid) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  print()
   fatty_acid n
1        14:0 4
2        15:0 3
3        16:1 3
4        12:0 2
5        13:0 2
6        16:0 2
7    18:2n-6c 2
8        22:1 2
9        22:2 2
10       24:0 2
11       18:1 1
12    18:3n-3 1
13       20:1 1
14    20:2n-6 1
15    20:3n-6 1
16       21:0 1
17       24:1 1
18        8:0 1

7 Fatty acid composition biofilm

Statistics for fatty acid composition of the biofilm in absolute values (µg fatty acid / mg dry weight biofilm).

# insert data
FA_Biofilm_absolute <-
  read.table(file.path(prj, "data\\FA_Biofilm_absolute.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(FA_Biofilm_absolute)

any(is.na(FA_Biofilm_absolute))
[1] FALSE

Following fatty acids were not considered for biofilm analysis due to too less values: C10:0, C11:0, C14:1.

Therefore, delete rows with respective fatty acids:

FA_Biofilm_absolute <- FA_Biofilm_absolute %>%
  filter(!fa %in% c("C10:0", "C11:0", "C14:1"))

Total SAFA, MUFA & PUFA content

1) Total SAFA content

# total SAFA, MUFA, PUFA content; ba = "biofilm absolute"
# total SAFA content
# filter saturated fatty acids in new table
SAFA_ba <- FA_Biofilm_absolute %>%
    filter(fa %in% c("C08:0", "C10:0", "C12:0", "C13:0", "C14:0", "C15:0", "C16:0", "C18:0", "C20:0", "C22:0", "C24:0"))

# calculate the total SAFA content per replicate
total_SAFA_per_replicate <- SAFA_ba %>%
  group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  summarize(sum_SAFA = sum(fa_conc))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'replicate'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(total_SAFA_per_replicate)
# A tibble: 12 × 3
# Groups:   replicate [12]
   replicate treatment sum_SAFA
   <chr>     <chr>        <dbl>
 1 B_1       NC            190.
 2 B_10      Cip           271.
 3 B_11      Pro           346.
 4 B_12      Mix           329.
 5 B_2       Cip           165.
 6 B_3       Pro           233.
 7 B_4       Mix           193.
 8 B_5       NC            240.
 9 B_6       Cip           190.
10 B_7       Pro           175.
11 B_8       Mix           138.
12 B_9       NC            274.
# mean
mean_SAFA_ba <- total_SAFA_per_replicate %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_SAFA = mean(sum_SAFA, na.rm = TRUE))
print(mean_SAFA_ba)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_SAFA
  <chr>         <dbl>
1 Cip            209.
2 Mix            220.
3 NC             235.
4 Pro            251.

2) Total MUFA content

# filter mono-unsaturated fatty acids in new table
MUFA_ba <- FA_Biofilm_absolute %>%
  filter(fa %in% c("C15:1", "C16:1", "C17:1", "C18:1", "C20:1","C22:1", "C24:1"))

# total MUFA content per replicate
total_MUFA_per_replicate <- MUFA_ba %>%
  group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  summarize(sum_MUFA = sum(fa_conc))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'replicate'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(total_MUFA_per_replicate)
# A tibble: 12 × 3
# Groups:   replicate [12]
   replicate treatment sum_MUFA
   <chr>     <chr>        <dbl>
 1 B_1       NC            72.5
 2 B_10      Cip          379. 
 3 B_11      Pro          504. 
 4 B_12      Mix          422. 
 5 B_2       Cip           77.6
 6 B_3       Pro           68.3
 7 B_4       Mix           67.0
 8 B_5       NC           139. 
 9 B_6       Cip           86.2
10 B_7       Pro          130. 
11 B_8       Mix           66.5
12 B_9       NC            45.0
# mean
mean_MUFA_ba <- total_MUFA_per_replicate %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_MUFA = mean(sum_MUFA, na.rm = TRUE))
print(mean_MUFA_ba)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_MUFA
  <chr>         <dbl>
1 Cip           181. 
2 Mix           185. 
3 NC             85.6
4 Pro           234.

3) Total PUFA content

# filter poly-unsaturated fatty acids in new table
PUFA_ba <- FA_Biofilm_absolute %>%
  filter(fa %in% c("C18:2(n-6c)", "C18:2(n-6t)", "C18:3(n-3)", "C18:3(n-6)", "C18:4(n-3)", "C20:2(n-6)", "C20:3(n-6)", "C20:3(n-3)", "C20:4(n-6)", "C20:5(n-3)", "C22:2", "C22:5(n-3)", "C22:6(n-3)"))
  
# calculate total PUFA content per replicate
total_PUFA_per_replicate <- PUFA_ba %>%
  group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  summarize(sum_PUFA = sum(fa_conc))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'replicate'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(total_PUFA_per_replicate)
# A tibble: 12 × 3
# Groups:   replicate [12]
   replicate treatment sum_PUFA
   <chr>     <chr>        <dbl>
 1 B_1       NC            205.
 2 B_10      Cip           265.
 3 B_11      Pro           123.
 4 B_12      Mix           304.
 5 B_2       Cip           191.
 6 B_3       Pro           182.
 7 B_4       Mix           221.
 8 B_5       NC            191.
 9 B_6       Cip           213.
10 B_7       Pro           145.
11 B_8       Mix           109.
12 B_9       NC            376.
# mean
mean_PUFA_ba <- total_PUFA_per_replicate %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_PUFA = mean(sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE))
print(mean_PUFA_ba)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_PUFA
  <chr>         <dbl>
1 Cip            223.
2 Mix            211.
3 NC             257.
4 Pro            150.

4) Total FA content

# total FA 
total_FA_biofilm <- bind_rows(total_SAFA_per_replicate, total_MUFA_per_replicate, total_PUFA_per_replicate)

total_FA_per_replicate <- total_FA_biofilm %>%
  group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  summarize(sum_FA = sum(sum_SAFA, sum_MUFA, sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'replicate'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(total_FA_per_replicate)
# A tibble: 12 × 3
# Groups:   replicate [12]
   replicate treatment sum_FA
   <chr>     <chr>      <dbl>
 1 B_1       NC          468.
 2 B_10      Cip         915.
 3 B_11      Pro         972.
 4 B_12      Mix        1054.
 5 B_2       Cip         434.
 6 B_3       Pro         483.
 7 B_4       Mix         480.
 8 B_5       NC          570.
 9 B_6       Cip         490.
10 B_7       Pro         450.
11 B_8       Mix         313.
12 B_9       NC          695.
# mean
mean_total_FA_ba <- total_FA_per_replicate %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(mean_FA = mean(sum_FA, na.rm = TRUE))
print(mean_total_FA_ba)
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  treatment mean_FA
  <chr>       <dbl>
1 Cip          613.
2 Mix          616.
3 NC           578.
4 Pro          635.

Histogram FA biofilm

hist(FA_Biofilm_absolute $ fa_conc) 

skewness(FA_Biofilm_absolute $ fa_conc, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 3.743203
shapiro.test(FA_Biofilm_absolute $ fa_conc)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  FA_Biofilm_absolute$fa_conc
W = 0.46919, p-value < 2.2e-16

Biofilm fatty acid concentration not normal distributed and right skewed.

NMDS FA biofilm

Change table to wide format:

# add prefix (week) 1,2 or 3 to treatments for later subgrouping
FA_Biofilm_absolute <- FA_Biofilm_absolute %>%
  mutate(treatment = if_else(week == "1", paste("1_", treatment, sep = ""), treatment))

FA_Biofilm_absolute <- FA_Biofilm_absolute %>%
  mutate(treatment = if_else(week == "2", paste("2_", treatment, sep = ""), treatment))

FA_Biofilm_absolute <- FA_Biofilm_absolute %>%
  mutate(treatment = if_else(week == "3", paste("3_", treatment, sep = ""), treatment))

# change table to wide format
FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide <-
  pivot_wider(FA_Biofilm_absolute,
              names_from = fa,
              values_from = fa_conc)

# square root transformation, to reduce discriminatory power of dominant fa groups
FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt <- sqrt(FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide[, -c(1, 2, 3)])
View(FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt)

any(is.na(FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt))
[1] FALSE

Perform metaMDS:

# Rename and ease col-names for later NMDS plot
FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt <- FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt %>%
  rename(
    `8:0`      = `C08:0`,   
    `12:0`     = `C12:0`,   
    `13:0`     = `C13:0`,
    `14:0`     = `C14:0`,
    `15:0`     = `C15:0`,
    `15:1`     = `C15:1`,
    `16:0`     = `C16:0`,
    `16:1`     = `C16:1`,
    `17:1`     = `C17:1`,
    `18:0`     = `C18:0`,
    `18:1`     = `C18:1`,
    `18:2n-6t` = `C18:2(n-6t)`,
    `18:2n-6c` = `C18:2(n-6c)`,
    `18:3n-6`  = `C18:3(n-6)`,
    `18:3n-3`  = `C18:3(n-3)`,
    `18:4n-3`  = `C18:4(n-3)`,
    `20:0`     = `C20:0`,
    `20:1`     = `C20:1`,
    `20:2n-6`  = `C20:2(n-6)`,
    `20:3n-6`  = `C20:3(n-6)`,
    `20:4n-6`  = `C20:4(n-6)`,
    `20:3n-3`  = `C20:3(n-3)`,
    `21:0`     = `C21:0`,
    `20:5n-3`  = `C20:5(n-3)`,
    `22:0`     = `C22:0`,
    `22:1`     = `C22:1`,
    `22:2`     = `C22:2`,
    `22:5n-3`  = `C22:5(n-3)`,
    `22:6n-3`  = `C22:6(n-3)`,
    `24:0`     = `C24:0`,
    `24:1`     = `C24:1`
  )



NMDS_Biofilm_absolute <-
  metaMDS(
    FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt,
    autotransform = FALSE,
    k = 2 ,
    distance = "bray",
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
Run 0 stress 0.06061942 
Run 1 stress 0.05346501 
... New best solution
... Procrustes: rmse 0.09407197  max resid 0.2413346 
Run 2 stress 0.05346507 
... Procrustes: rmse 0.0001877997  max resid 0.0004732888 
... Similar to previous best
Run 3 stress 0.05346505 
... Procrustes: rmse 0.0001846581  max resid 0.0004655489 
... Similar to previous best
Run 4 stress 0.05346501 
... Procrustes: rmse 0.0001400711  max resid 0.0003523907 
... Similar to previous best
Run 5 stress 0.05346501 
... New best solution
... Procrustes: rmse 0.0001013094  max resid 0.0002552878 
... Similar to previous best
Run 6 stress 0.3473053 
Run 7 stress 0.0606196 
Run 8 stress 0.06734583 
Run 9 stress 0.053465 
... New best solution
... Procrustes: rmse 2.731125e-05  max resid 5.799487e-05 
... Similar to previous best
Run 10 stress 0.07016022 
Run 11 stress 0.06795755 
Run 12 stress 0.0727874 
Run 13 stress 0.05346506 
... Procrustes: rmse 3.97717e-05  max resid 9.193791e-05 
... Similar to previous best
Run 14 stress 0.1823399 
Run 15 stress 0.05346505 
... Procrustes: rmse 9.862544e-05  max resid 0.0002483217 
... Similar to previous best
Run 16 stress 0.05346503 
... Procrustes: rmse 0.0001181591  max resid 0.000296388 
... Similar to previous best
Run 17 stress 0.07341763 
Run 18 stress 0.06061969 
Run 19 stress 0.053465 
... New best solution
... Procrustes: rmse 2.287863e-05  max resid 4.793897e-05 
... Similar to previous best
Run 20 stress 0.07016023 
*** Best solution repeated 1 times
NMDS_Biofilm_absolute$stress
[1] 0.053465

Create fa vectors

# separation in SAFA; MUFA & PUFA to build vectors in NMDS
# "ba" = biofilm absolute
FA_variables.SAFAs_ba <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Biofilm_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "8:0",
        "12:0",
        "13:0",
        "14:0",
        "15:0",
        "16:0",
        "18:0",
        "20:0",
        "21:0",
        "22:0",
        "24:0"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

FA_variables.MUFAs_ba <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Biofilm_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "15:1",
        "16:1",
        "17:1",
        "18:1",
        "20:1",
        "22:1",
        "24:1"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

FA_variables.PUFAs_ba <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Biofilm_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "18:2n-6t",
        "18:2n-6c",
        "18:3n-6",
        "18:3n-3",
        "18:4n-3",
        "20:2n-6",
        "20:3n-6",
        "20:4n-6",
        "20:3n-3",
        "20:5n-3",
        "22:2",
        "22:5n-3",
        "22:6n-3"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

# Extract NMDS scores
data.scores_biofilm_absolute = as.data.frame(scores(NMDS_Biofilm_absolute)$sites)

Treatments <-
  FA_Biofilm_absolute$treatment[FA_Biofilm_absolute$fa == "C16:0"]

# add treatment column to NMDS scores
data.scores_biofilm_absolute$Treatment = Treatments

# vectors for SAFA, MUFA and PUFA
SAFAs_coord_cont_ba = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.SAFAs_ba, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.SAFAs_ba)
MUFAs_coord_cont_ba = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.MUFAs_ba, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.MUFAs_ba)
PUFAs_coord_cont_ba = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.PUFAs_ba, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.PUFAs_ba)

# create weeks as subgroups for plotting
data.scores_biofilm_absolute <- data.scores_biofilm_absolute %>%
  mutate(time.point = case_when(
    str_detect(Treatment, "^1_") ~ "Week1",
    str_detect(Treatment, "^2_") ~ "Week2",
    str_detect(Treatment, "^3_") ~ "Week3",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))
# first delete 1_, 2_ & 3_ prefix in treatments
data.scores_biofilm_absolute $ Treatment <- sub("1_", "", data.scores_biofilm_absolute $ Treatment)
data.scores_biofilm_absolute $ Treatment <- sub("2_", "", data.scores_biofilm_absolute $ Treatment)
data.scores_biofilm_absolute $ Treatment <- sub("3_", "", data.scores_biofilm_absolute $ Treatment)

Plot NMDS:

# Reorder treatment variable for the plot
data.scores_biofilm_absolute$Treatment <- factor(
  data.scores_biofilm_absolute$Treatment,
  levels = c("NC", "Cip", "Pro", "Mix")
)

# Compute convex hull for each Treatment
polygon_data_biofilm_treatment <- data.scores_biofilm_absolute %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(hull_points = list(data.frame(
    NMDS1 = NMDS1[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)],
    NMDS2 = NMDS2[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)]
  ))) %>%
  unnest(hull_points)

# plot
absol_biofilm_with_vectors = ggplot(data = data.scores_biofilm_absolute, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
    geom_polygon(
    data = polygon_data_biofilm_treatment,
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = Treatment, fill = Treatment),
    alpha = 0.2,
    show.legend = FALSE  
  ) +
  geom_point( aes(shape = time.point, color = Treatment),
    size = 5,
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) +
  labs(shape = "Week", color = "Treatment") +
  geom_segment(data = PUFAs_coord_cont_ba, aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1 * 0.3, yend = NMDS2 * 0.3), linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_segment(data = MUFAs_coord_cont_ba, aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1 * 0.2, yend = NMDS2 * 0.2), linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_segment(data = SAFAs_coord_cont_ba, aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1 * 0.3, yend = NMDS2 * 0.3), linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = PUFAs_coord_cont_ba, 
    aes(x = NMDS1 * 0.3, y = NMDS2 * 0.3, label = row.names(PUFAs_coord_cont_ba)), 
    colour = "grey30",
    size = 3) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = MUFAs_coord_cont_ba, 
    aes(x = NMDS1 * 0.2, y = NMDS2 * 0.2, label = row.names(MUFAs_coord_cont_ba)), 
    colour = "grey30",
    size = 3) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = SAFAs_coord_cont_ba, 
    aes(x = NMDS1 * 0.3, y = NMDS2 * 0.3, label = row.names(SAFAs_coord_cont_ba)), 
    colour = "grey30",
    size = 3) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey30"),
    plot.margin = margin(
      t = 0.2,
      b = 0.2,
      l = 0.2,
      r = 0.2,
      unit = "cm"
    ),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 
  
  
absol_biofilm_with_vectors

# position of FA names manually adjusted in vector graphic

Save the plot as .png:

ggsave(absol_biofilm_with_vectors, 
       filename = "NMDS_Biofilm_absolute.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image

Save the plot as vector graphic:

ggsave(plot = absol_biofilm_with_vectors,
       "NMDS_Biofilm_absolute.svg", 
       device = "svg",
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image

Now plot again without vectors and treatment polygon:

absol_biofilm_treatment_polygon = ggplot(data = data.scores_biofilm_absolute, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
    geom_polygon(
    data = polygon_data_biofilm_treatment,
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = Treatment, fill = Treatment),
    alpha = 0.2,
    show.legend = FALSE  
  ) +
  geom_point( aes(shape = time.point, color = Treatment),
    size = 5,
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) +
  labs(shape = "Week", color = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey30"),
    plot.margin = margin(
      t = 0.2,
      b = 0.2,
      l = 0.2,
      r = 0.2,
      unit = "cm"
    ),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 
  
  
absol_biofilm_treatment_polygon

Save the plot as .png:

ggsave(absol_biofilm_treatment_polygon, 
       filename = "NMDS_Biofilm_treatment_polygon.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image

Now plot with week polygon:

# Compute convex hull for each week
polygon_data_biofilm_week <- data.scores_biofilm_absolute %>%
  group_by(time.point) %>%
  summarise(hull_points = list(data.frame(
    NMDS1 = NMDS1[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)],
    NMDS2 = NMDS2[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)]
  ))) %>%
  unnest(hull_points)

# Plot
absol_biofilm_week_polygon = ggplot(data = data.scores_biofilm_absolute, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
    geom_polygon(
    data = polygon_data_biofilm_week,
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = time.point, fill = time.point),
    alpha = 0.2,
    show.legend = FALSE  
  ) +
  geom_point( aes(shape = time.point, color = Treatment),
    size = 5,
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NC" = "#009E73", "Pro" = "#E69F00", "Cip" = "#56B4E9", "Mix" = "#F0E442")) +
  labs(shape = "Week", color = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey30"),
    plot.margin = margin(
      t = 0.2,
      b = 0.2,
      l = 0.2,
      r = 0.2,
      unit = "cm"
    ),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 
  
  
absol_biofilm_week_polygon

Save the plot as .png:

ggsave(absol_biofilm_week_polygon, 
       filename = "NMDS_Biofilm_week_polygon.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image

PERMANOVA FA biofilm

Create distance matrix with fatty acids:

FA_biofilm_dist <- vegdist(FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt, method = 'bray')

Separate explanatory variables from fatty acid information:

FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova <-
  pivot_wider(FA_Biofilm_absolute,
              names_from = fa,
              values_from = fa_conc)

# square root transformation again, to reduce discriminatory power of dominant fa groups
FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova[, 4:34] <- sqrt(FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova[, 4:34])

# delete fa columns
FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova <- FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova[, c(1,2, 3)]
FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova $ week <- as.factor(FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova $ week)
FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova $ treatment <- as.factor(FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova $ treatment)

Perform PERMANOVA for treatments:

# first remove 1_, 2_, 3_ and prefixes
FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment <- gsub("^[1-3]_","", FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova$treatment)

# perform PERMANOVA for treatments
perm_FA_biofilm_treatment <- adonis2(FA_biofilm_dist ~ treatment, data = FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
print(perm_FA_biofilm_treatment)
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
Terms added sequentially (first to last)
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999

adonis2(formula = FA_biofilm_dist ~ treatment, data = FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
treatment  3 0.037007 0.13958 0.4326  0.913
Residual   8 0.228117 0.86042              
Total     11 0.265124 1.00000

No significant differences between biofilm treatmens. Anyway pairwise comparisons (by: Martinez Arbizu, P. (2020). pairwiseAdonis: Pairwise multilevel comparison using adonis. R package version 0.4):

pairwise_results_biofilm_treatment <- pairwise.adonis2(
  FA_biofilm_dist ~ treatment,
  data = FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova,
  permutations = 99
)

# View the results
print(pairwise_results_biofilm_treatment)
$parent_call

          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)

Total      5 0.090292 1.00000

Total      5 0.138413 1.00000

          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)

Total      5 0.159202 1.00000              

attr(,"class")
[1] "pwadstrata" "list"

No significant differences. Now PERMANOVA for week variable:

perm_FA_biofilm_week <- adonis2(FA_biofilm_dist ~ week, data = FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
print(perm_FA_biofilm_week)
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
Terms added sequentially (first to last)
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999

adonis2(formula = FA_biofilm_dist ~ week, data = FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
         Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)   
week      2  0.14802 0.55831 5.6882  0.005 **
Residual  9  0.11710 0.44169                 
Total    11  0.26512 1.00000                 
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Now pairwise tests:

pairwise_results_biofilm_week <- pairwise.adonis2(
  FA_biofilm_dist ~ week,
  data = FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova,
  permutations = 99
)

# View the results
print(pairwise_results_biofilm_week)
$parent_call
[1] "FA_biofilm_dist ~ week , strata = Null , permutations 999"

$`1_vs_2`
         Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)  
week      1 0.021157 0.29093 2.4618   0.04 *
Residual  6 0.051566 0.70907                
Total     7 0.072723 1.00000                
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

$`1_vs_3`
         Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)  
week      1 0.105706 0.57206 8.0205   0.05 *
Residual  6 0.079077 0.42794                
Total     7 0.184783 1.00000                
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

$`2_vs_3`
         Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)   
week      1  0.09517 0.47889 5.5138   0.01 **
Residual  6  0.10356 0.52111                 
Total     7  0.19873 1.00000                 
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

attr(,"class")
[1] "pwadstrata" "list"

All weeks different, as already indicated by NMDS.

SIMPER FA biofilm

First SIMPER for treatments:

# create extra table with treatments
biofilm_treatments_table <- FA_biofilm_absolute_wide_permanova

View(biofilm_treatments_table)

# Perform SIMPER
simper_FA_biofilm_treatments <- with(biofilm_treatments_table, simper(FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt, treatment,  permutations = 999))

summary(simper_FA_biofilm_treatments)

---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999
# Combine results into a data frame
simper_results_biofilm_treatments <- do.call(rbind, lapply(simper_FA_biofilm_treatments, function(x) {
  # Check if the element contains valid data
  if (!is.null(x$p)) {
    data.frame(
      fatty_acid = names(x$p),  
      p = as.numeric(x$p),      
      average = as.numeric(x$average),  
      sd = as.numeric(x$sd),
      ratio = as.numeric(x$ratio),
      cusum = as.numeric(x$cusum),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    NULL  # Skip if no p-values
  }
}))


# Show only significant results
simper_results_biofilm_treatments %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::select(fatty_acid, p, average, sd, ratio, cusum)  
          fatty_acid     p     average          sd    ratio     cusum
NC_Cip.2        12:0 0.012 0.003450946 0.002011797 1.715355 0.2503988
NC_Cip.22    20:3n-3 0.047 0.009153171 0.004632816 1.975725 0.9454872
NC_Pro.10       18:0 0.033 0.017402772 0.011749821 1.481110 0.7652682
NC_Pro.22    20:3n-3 0.050 0.008782707 0.004653172 1.887467 0.9549713
Cip_Pro.2       12:0 0.036 0.003300774 0.001830190 1.803515 0.3796463
# How often do they occur
simper_results_biofilm_treatments %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::count(fatty_acid) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  print()
  fatty_acid n
1       12:0 2
2    20:3n-3 2
3       18:0 1

Now perform SIMPER for weeks:

simper_FA_biofilm_week <- with(biofilm_treatments_table, simper(FA_Biofilm_absolute_wide_sqrt, week,  permutations = 999))

summary(simper_FA_biofilm_week)

Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999
# Combine results into a data frame
simper_results_biofilm_week <- do.call(rbind, lapply(simper_FA_biofilm_week, function(x) {
  # Check if the element contains valid data
  if (!is.null(x$p)) {
    data.frame(
      fatty_acid = names(x$p),  
      p = as.numeric(x$p),      
      average = as.numeric(x$average),  
      sd = as.numeric(x$sd),
      ratio = as.numeric(x$ratio),
      cusum = as.numeric(x$cusum),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    NULL  # Skip if no p-values
  }
}))


# Show only significant results
simper_results_biofilm_week %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::select(fatty_acid, p, average, sd, ratio, cusum)  
       fatty_acid     p      average           sd     ratio     cusum

# How often do they occur
simper_results_biofilm_week %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::count(fatty_acid) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  print()
   fatty_acid n
1        14:0 2
2        16:0 2
3        18:1 2
4     18:3n-3 2
5     18:4n-3 2
6        22:1 2

11   18:2n-6t 1

20       24:0 1

8 Temperature and light conditions

8.1 Biofilm colonisation and contamination

# insert data
colonisation_temp_light <-
  read.table(file.path(prj, "data\\colonisation_temp_light.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(colonisation_temp_light)

# average temperature
mean(colonisation_temp_light $ temperature_colonisation)
[1] 18.28751
# 18.28751 °C

# mean light intensity
# filter only daytime
average_light_intensity_colonisation <- colonisation_temp_light %>%
  dplyr::filter(light_intensity_colonisation > 0) %>%
  summarize(average_light_intensity_colonisation = mean(light_intensity_colonisation))

print(average_light_intensity_colonisation)
  average_light_intensity_colonisation
1                             14019.26
# 14019.26 lux

# temperature plot
# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
colonisation_temp_light $ temperature_colonisation <- jitter(colonisation_temp_light $ temperature_colonisation)

t2 <- ggplot(
  data = colonisation_temp_light,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = temperature_colonisation, color = time_of_day)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth() +
  labs(
    title = "Temperature profile during biofilm colonisation",
    x = "Day", y = "Temperature (°C)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 77, by = 5)) +
  scale_color_continuous(
    name = "Time of Day",  
    breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20),
    labels = c("08:00 pm", "03:00 pm", "10:00 am", "05:00 am", "0:00 am"),
    limits = c(0, 22),
  )

t2 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'
Warning: The following aesthetics were dropped during statistical transformation: colour
ℹ This can happen when ggplot fails to infer the correct grouping structure in
  the data.
ℹ Did you forget to specify a `group` aesthetic or to convert a numerical
  variable into a factor?

# save the plot
ggsave(t2, 
       filename = "temperature_colonisation.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'
Warning: The following aesthetics were dropped during statistical transformation: colour
ℹ This can happen when ggplot fails to infer the correct grouping structure in
  the data.
ℹ Did you forget to specify a `group` aesthetic or to convert a numerical
  variable into a factor?
# light intensity plot
# Filter the data to include only rows where light_intensity > 0
filtered_data2 <- colonisation_temp_light %>% 
  filter(light_intensity_colonisation > 0)

# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
filtered_data2 $ light_intensity_colonisation <- jitter(filtered_data2 $ light_intensity_colonisation)

l2 <- ggplot(
  data = filtered_data2,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = light_intensity_colonisation)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth(color = "#E69F00", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Light intensity profile during biofilm colonisation",
    x = "Day", y = "Light intensity (lux)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 77, by = 5)) 


l2 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'

# save the plot
ggsave(l2, 
       filename = "light_intensity_colonisation.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'

8.2 Feeding assay

# insert data
feeding_assay_temp_light <-
  read.table(file.path(prj, "data\\feeding_assay_temp_light.txt"),
             header = TRUE, 
             sep = "\t"
  )
             
View(feeding_assay_temp_light)

# average temperature
mean(feeding_assay_temp_light $ temperature)
[1] 13.27927
# 13.27927 °C

# mean light intensity
# filter only daytime
average_light_intensity_feeding <- feeding_assay_temp_light %>%
  dplyr::filter(light_intensity > 0) %>%
  summarize(average_light_intensity_feeding = mean(light_intensity))

print(average_light_intensity_feeding)
  average_light_intensity_feeding
1                        330.3552
# 330.3552 lux

# temperature plot
# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
feeding_assay_temp_light $ temperature <- jitter(feeding_assay_temp_light $ temperature)

t1 <- ggplot(
  data = feeding_assay_temp_light,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = temperature, color = time_of_day)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth() +
  labs(
    title = "Temperature profile during 21-day feeding assay",
    x = "Day", y = "Temperature (°C)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:21) +
  scale_color_continuous(
    name = "Time of Day",  
    breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20),
    labels = c("08:00 pm", "03:00 pm", "10:00 am", "05:00 am", "0:00 am"),
    limits = c(0, 22),
  )

t1 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'
Warning: The following aesthetics were dropped during statistical transformation: colour
ℹ This can happen when ggplot fails to infer the correct grouping structure in
  the data.
ℹ Did you forget to specify a `group` aesthetic or to convert a numerical
  variable into a factor?

# save the plot
ggsave(t1, 
       filename = "temperature_feeding_assay.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
Saving 7 x 5 in image
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'

ℹ This can happen when ggplot fails to infer the correct grouping structure in
  the data.

  variable into a factor?
# light intensity plot
# Filter the data to include only rows where light_intensity > 0
filtered_data1 <- feeding_assay_temp_light %>% 
  filter(light_intensity > 0)

# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
filtered_data1 $ light_intensity <- jitter(filtered_data1 $ light_intensity)

l1 <- ggplot(
  data = filtered_data1,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = light_intensity)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth(color = "#E69F00", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Light intensity profile during 21-day feeding assay",
    x = "Day", y = "Light intensity (lux)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:21) 


l1 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'

# save the plot
ggsave(l1, 
       filename = "light_intensity_feeding_assay.png", 
       path = 'C:\\Users\\Oster Test\\OneDrive\\Desktop\\Promotion\\Bioassays\\Schneckenversuch 2023\\Bottom_up_effects_biofilm_grazer\\output')
